content:
# 单篇讲义生成任务

项目仓库: BerkeleyLab/Bedrock
项目名: Bedrock
讲义目录: Bedrock-tutorial/
代码永久链接 base: https://github.com/BerkeleyLab/Bedrock/blob/235f3e3b5602790927caf62a405fce81213bb3de/
当前 HEAD: 235f3e3b5602790927caf62a405fce81213bb3de
动作: new


---

## 项目学习路线（大纲方法论）

# 项目学习手册·大纲规划 Planner

## 角色

你是资深技术教育专家、源码阅读导师和项目学习路线设计者。

你运行在被分析项目的根目录内。

你可以使用：

- `Read`
- `Grep`
- `Glob`
- 只读 `git` 命令，例如 `git log`、`git diff`、`git show`、`git ls-files`

你不能：

- 修改源码
- 写入任何 `.md` 文件
- 输出 JSON 以外的文字

你的唯一产出是一个学习手册大纲 manifest JSON。

---

## 总目标

为当前项目规划一套「从零开始、由浅入深、结合源码和代码实践」的学习手册。

这套手册会被拆分成多篇 Markdown 讲义，后续 worker 会根据 manifest 逐篇生成。

每篇讲义应该满足：

1. 面向初学者也能读懂。
2. 只讲一个相对独立的主题。
3. 必须结合真实源码。
4. 必须包含一个可操作的代码实践任务。
5. 讲义之间有清晰依赖关系。

---

## 源码探查顺序

请按以下顺序理解项目，不要一开始就深入细节：

1. 项目说明
   - README
   - docs
   - examples
   - tutorials

2. 构建与运行方式
   - package.json
   - Cargo.toml
   - pyproject.toml
   - go.mod
   - pom.xml
   - Makefile
   - Dockerfile
   - CI 配置

3. 项目入口
   - CLI 入口
   - Web 服务入口
   - main 文件
   - library export 文件
   - examples 入口

4. 目录结构
   - src
   - lib
   - app
   - packages
   - crates
   - tests

5. 核心流程
   - 初始化流程
   - 配置加载
   - 请求/任务/数据处理主链路
   - 插件、扩展、调度、存储等核心机制

6. 测试与示例
   - 单元测试
   - 集成测试
   - benchmark
   - example usage

---

## 学习路线设计原则

请把学习手册设计成三层：

1. 入门层 beginner
   目标：让读者知道项目是什么、怎么运行、目录如何组织、核心入口在哪里。

2. 进阶层 intermediate
   目标：让读者理解主要模块、核心调用链、关键数据结构和配置机制。

3. 专家层 advanced
   目标：让读者理解扩展点、性能、并发、错误处理、测试、二次开发和架构取舍。

---

## 讲义拆分规则

- 每篇讲义只解决一个主要问题。
- 每篇讲义包含 2 到 5 个最小模块。
- 每篇讲义必须有一个 `practice_task`，描述读者要完成的代码实践。
- 每篇讲义的源码范围要适中，通常引用 1 到 6 个关键源码文件。
- 大型项目可以拆成 30 篇以上讲义；中型项目 15 到 30 篇；小型项目 8 到 15 篇。
- 不要为了数量硬拆，逻辑优先。
- 第一单元必须帮助读者从零开始，包括项目定位、运行方式、目录结构、入口文件。
- 后续单元再进入核心模块、源码机制、扩展实践。

---

## mode=full

首次生成大纲时：

1. 阅读项目关键文件。
2. 理解项目定位、技术栈、运行方式和核心模块。
3. 设计从零开始的学习路径。
4. 输出完整 manifest。
5. 所有讲义的 `action` 都必须是 `"new"`。

---

## mode=incremental

增量更新时：

1. 读取 stdin 中提供的现有 manifest。
2. 阅读项目中已有的 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 讲义目录，理解旧结构。
3. 使用：

   - `git diff previous_head..current_head`
   - `git log`
   - `git show`

   分析代码变化。

4. 输出更新后的完整 manifest。

每篇讲义的 `action` 按以下规则设置：

- `"keep"`
  代码变化不影响这篇讲义，无需重写。

- `"update"`
  小范围改动，例如函数重命名、参数调整、局部逻辑变化、链接行号变化。

- `"new"`
  新增功能、新增模块、新增重要机制，需要新增讲义。

- `"rebuild"`
  架构、主流程、公共接口、核心模型发生重大变化，旧讲义需要重建。

如果某篇旧讲义已经不再适合当前项目结构，可以从 manifest 中移除。

---

## manifest 输出格式

只输出严格 JSON，不要输出 Markdown，不要输出解释文字。

```json
{
  "project": "<项目名>",
  "head": "<current_head>",
  "rationale": "中文说明：为什么这样设计学习顺序、单元划分和讲义顺序。",
  "units": [
    {
      "id": "u1",
      "title": "单元标题",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 1,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u1-l1",
          "title": "讲义标题",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u1-l1-project-overview.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "本讲义要讲什么，用 1 到 3 句中文说明。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "学习目标 1",
            "学习目标 2"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "src/main.ts"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "模块 1",
            "模块 2"
          ],
          "practice_task": "本讲义对应的代码实践任务。",
          "depends_on": [],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## 字段要求

- `project`：项目名。
- `head`：当前 git HEAD。
- `rationale`：中文，一段即可。
- `unit.id`：格式为 `u1`、`u2`、`u3`。
- `lecture.id`：格式为 `u1-l1`、`u1-l2`。
- `filename`：
  - 必须全局唯一。
  - 必须小写。
  - 使用连字符。
  - 必须以 `.md` 结尾。
  - 文件位于 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 目录下。
- `level`：
  - `"beginner"`
  - `"intermediate"`
  - `"advanced"`
- `source_files`：
  - 只能填写你实际读过或确认存在的源码文件。
  - 不要编造文件路径。
- `depends_on`：
  - 使用讲义 id。
  - 没有依赖时填写空数组。
- `action`：
  - full 模式全部为 `"new"`。
  - incremental 模式使用 `"keep"`、`"update"`、`"new"`、`"rebuild"`。

---

## 重要约束

- 只基于真实源码和项目文件规划。
- 不确定的地方写 `"待确认"`。
- 不要编造不存在的模块、命令、接口或源码文件。
- 输出必须是合法 JSON。


## 完整大纲

```json
{
  "project": "Bedrock",
  "rationale": "Bedrock 是 LBNL 多年积累的 Verilog HDL 库，体量大、子系统多（dsp/cordic/badger/localbus/rtsim/cmoc/soc/serial_io/projects 等），但有一条清晰的主线：以 localbus 为片上互联、以 Make+iverilog 为统一仿真测试方法、以 newad.py 自动生成寄存器地址映射，在其上构建 DSP/RF/以太网/串行链路/SoC 等子系统。因此学习顺序遵循「先定位与运行项目 → 掌握通用方法学（Make 测试、localbus、寄存器映射）→ 由浅入深学 DSP 基础模块 → 进入时钟域与片上互联/以太网 → 高速串行链路 → 验证与 RF 系统级设计 → 最后 SoC 与平台集成」。单元 1-2 为入门层，帮助读者从零认识项目并跑通第一个测试；单元 3-5 为进阶层，覆盖构成大多数 Bedrock 设计的核心可复用模块；单元 6-7 为专家层，进入形式化验证、RF 系统仿真与控制、软核 SoC 及多板卡工程集成。每篇讲义只讲一个主题、绑定 1~6 个真实源码文件，并给出一个可动手的代码实践任务，讲义之间通过 depends_on 形成由浅入深的依赖链。",
  "units": [
    {
      "id": "u1",
      "title": "认识 Bedrock：项目定位、运行方式与编码规范",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 1,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u1-l1",
          "title": "项目总览：Bedrock 是什么",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u1-l1-project-overview.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "介绍 Bedrock 的定位：LBNL 多年积累的、平台无关的 Verilog HDL 库，用于 DSP/RF 控制与 FPGA 实现，以及它由哪些子系统组合而成。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 Bedrock 的定位、来源（LBNL）与应用场景（LLRF/光通信/DSP）",
            "记住顶层划分的主要子目录及其职责（dsp/cordic/badger/localbus/rtsim/cmoc/soc/projects 等）",
            "了解 Bedrock 的图形模式与批处理（命令行 make/grep/python）并存的设计哲学"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "dir_list.mk",
            "LICENSE.md"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "README 顶层说明",
            "dir_list.mk 目录常量定义"
          ],
          "practice_task": "阅读 README.md，整理出一张表格：列出 dsp、cordic、badger、localbus、rtsim、cmoc、soc、projects 各自负责什么，并用一句话写出你最感兴趣的子系统及原因。",
          "depends_on": [],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u1-l2",
          "title": "构建与运行：工具链与 selftest",
          "order": 2,
          "filename": "u1-l2-build-and-run.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解运行 Bedrock 所需的依赖（make/iverilog/python3、可选 verilator/yosys/gtkwave），以及如何用 selftest.sh 一键跑通本地测试子集。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "列出 Bedrock 的必需与推荐依赖",
            "理解 selftest.sh 的作用：覆盖 GitLab CI 的测试阶段并本地复现",
            "学会查看各子系统的 make 测试入口"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "selftest.sh",
            "dependencies.txt",
            "README.md"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "selftest.sh 测试编排",
            "dependencies.txt 依赖清单"
          ],
          "practice_task": "安装 make/iverilog/python3-numpy 后，运行 `sh selftest.sh`（或至少 `make -C cordic clean all`），记录哪些子测试 PASS、哪些因缺少工具被跳过，并解释跳过原因。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u1-l3",
          "title": "目录结构与代码导航",
          "order": 3,
          "filename": "u1-l3-directory-structure.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "带读者走读 Bedrock 的目录布局：源码（.v）、测试台（_tb.v）、波形配置（.gtkw）、Python 脚本与 Makefile 的命名约定，以及如何快速定位某个模块。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "掌握 *_tb.v、*.gtkw、rules.mk、top_rules.mk 等文件的命名与作用约定",
            "理解 dir_list.mk 如何用绝对路径变量串联各子目录",
            "学会用 grep/glob 在大型 Verilog 库中定位模块定义与实例"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "dir_list.mk",
            "cordic/Makefile",
            "dsp/Makefile"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "dir_list.mk 目录变量",
            "子目录 Makefile"
          ],
          "practice_task": "在 dsp/ 下找到 `mixer` 模块的定义文件、它的测试台文件和波形配置文件各是哪一个，并说明你用什么方式（文件名约定或搜索）定位到的。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u1-l4",
          "title": "RTL 编码规范",
          "order": 4,
          "filename": "u1-l4-rtl-guidelines.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "讲解 Bedrock 的 RTL 编码指南（guidelines/rtl_guidelines.md）：接口与信号命名、寄存/延时信号后缀、模块声明与参数、实例化约定等，使代码风格统一可读。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "掌握接口信号同前缀、snake_case、_n/_r/_d/_l 等命名后缀约定",
            "理解参数命名（全大写 snake_case）与默认值、按名连接端口、i_ 前缀实例名等规则",
            "明白这些约定如何降低跨模块理解成本、促进复用"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "guidelines/rtl_guidelines.md",
            "dsp/mixer.v",
            "cordic/cordic_wrap.v"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "rtl_guidelines.md 全部条目",
            "mixer.v 作为命名示例"
          ],
          "practice_task": "任选 dsp/ 下一个模块，按规范检查它的接口前缀、参数命名与默认值是否合规，写一份 5 条以内的符合/不符合清单；若发现不符，给出建议改名。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "u2",
      "title": "核心方法学：Make 测试、localbus 与寄存器映射",
      "level": "intermediate",
      "order": 2,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u2-l1",
          "title": "基于 Make 的 HDL 仿真测试方法",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u2-l1-make-hdl-testing.md",
          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "深入 Bedrock 的 Make 构建系统：top_rules.mk 中的模式规则（%_tb/%_check/%_view/%.vcd）、iverilog 编译与 vvp 仿真、VFLAGS 按目标定制，以及依赖推导。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 %_tb、%_check、%_view、%.vcd、%_lint 等模式规则如何驱动 iverilog 仿真",
            "学会用 VFLAGS_$@ / CFLAGS_$@ 等机制为单个目标定制参数",
            "掌握用 iverilog -y/-M 做依赖推导，并理解 top_rules.mk 如何被各目录 include 复用"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "build-tools/top_rules.mk",
            "build-tools/makefile.md",
            "build-tools/Makefile",
            "dsp/rules.mk"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "top_rules.mk 模式规则",
            "makefile.md 教程",
            "dsp/rules.mk 子目录规则"
          ],
          "practice_task": "在 dsp/ 目录运行 `make mixer_tb`、`make mixer_check`、`make mixer.vcd`，观察三个 target 分别调用了什么命令；再用 `make VFLAGS_mixer_tb=-DXXX mixer_tb` 验证按目标定制参数是否生效。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l2"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u2-l2",
          "title": "片上 localbus 总线",
          "order": 2,
          "filename": "u2-l2-localbus.md",
          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "讲解 Bedrock 贯穿全局的轻量 localbus：24 位地址/32 位读写数据、strobe 与读否信号、无握手无等待状态的设计取舍，以及 localbus.vh 仿真任务。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 localbus 的信号集（lb_clk/lb_addr/lb_wdata/lb_rdata/lb_strobe/lb_rnw）与无等待状态的取舍",
            "学会用 localbus.vh 的 lb_write_task/lb_read_task 编写周期精确的总线测试",
            "明白为何 Bedrock 拒绝 AXI/Wishbone 的握手机制"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "localbus/README.md",
            "localbus/localbus.vh",
            "localbus/tgen.v",
            "localbus/gen_regmap.py"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "localbus.vh 仿真任务",
            "README 总线说明",
            "tgen 写序列器"
          ],
          "practice_task": "阅读 localbus.vh，编写一个最小测试台片段，用 lb_write_task 向某地址写 0x1234，再用 lb_read_task 读回并断言一致（可基于 tgen_tb.v 改写）。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u1-l3"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u2-l3",
          "title": "寄存器地址映射自动生成 newad.py",
          "order": 3,
          "filename": "u2-l3-register-map-newad.md",
          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "讲解 build-tools/newad.py：通过 Verilog 中的 magic 注释标记 external 端口，自动生成地址映射、总线解码器与 JSON 寄存器表，大幅减少样板代码。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 newad.py 的输入（顶层 Verilog + 目录列表）与输出（地址头文件、解码器、JSON regmap）",
            "学会识别 external/external register 等 magic 注释及其含义",
            "掌握 newad.py 的主要 CLI 参数（-i/-o/-d/-r/-w/-b）与典型 Makefile 调用"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "build-tools/newad.md",
            "build-tools/newad.py",
            "build-tools/parser.py",
            "projects/ctrace/wctrace_top_regmap.json"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "newad.md 工作流说明",
            "newad.py 解析器",
            "parser.py Verilog 解析"
          ],
          "practice_task": "阅读 newad.md 的 magic 注释示例，在一个最小 Verilog 顶层里给两个端口加上 external 注释，运行 newad.py（参考其 -h）生成地址映射 JSON 并解读其中的地址分配。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l2"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "u3",
      "title": "DSP 基础模块",
      "level": "intermediate",
      "order": 3,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u3-l1",
          "title": "CORDIC 核：Python 生成的全展开流水线",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u3-l1-cordic.md",
          "level": "intermediate",
          "topic": "讲解 cordic 子系统：由 cordicgx.py 按数据位宽生成全展开的 cordicg_bN.v，支持极坐标↔直角坐标与 follow 模式，参数化 width/nstg/def_op。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "理解 CORDIC 原理与 Bedrock 的全展开流水线实现（每级一个 cstageg）",
            "掌握 cordicgx.py 如何按 DPW 生成 cordicg_bN.v 及对应 Makefile 规则",
            "理解 op 端口的四种模式（旋转/向量/follow）与精度/资源权衡"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "cordic/README",
            "cordic/Makefile",
            "cordic/cordicgx.py",
            "cordic/cstageg.v",
            "cordic/addsubg.v",
            "cordic/cordic_wrap.v"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "cordicgx.py 生成器",
            "cstageg.v 单级",
            "cordicg_tb 测试",
            "cordic_check.py 校验"
          ],
          "practice_task": "运行 `make -C cordic clean all`，观察 cordicgx.py 生成的 cordicg_b22.v，再用 `make -C cordic cordicg_b22.v DPW=18` 改变位宽重新生成，对比模块名与端口宽度变化。",
          "depends_on": [
            "u2-l1"
          ],
          "action": "new"
        },
        {
          "id": "u3-l2",
          "title": "混频器、DDS 与复数乘法",
          "order": 2,

## 前置讲义摘要（你必须基于这些已建立的认知继续，不要重复，要承接）

### u1-l1 项目总览：Bedrock 是什么

本讲是 Bedrock 学习手册的开篇，回答三个问题：Bedrock 是什么、由哪些部分组成、从哪里看起。结论是：Bedrock 是 LBNL 多年积累的、平台无关的 Verilog 代码库，用于 DSP 与 RF（含 LLRF）控制，并附带到 FPGA（如 Xilinx）所需的全部适配层。全讲只涉及仓库根目录两个最小模块：README.md 的顶层说明，以及 dir_list.mk 的目录常量定义。Bedrock 是一个多子系统聚合体，主要子系统包括 dsp（数字信号处理算法）、cordic（坐标旋转计算）、rtsim（RF 系统实时仿真）、cmoc（RF 控制器）、badger（响应 Ethernet/IP/UDP 的硬件核）、localbus（片上总线）、fpga_family 与 board_support（厂家与板卡适配）、projects（具体上板工程）等；soc/picorv32（RISC-V 软核 SoC）、serial_io、peripheral_drivers、build-tools 等仅见于 dir_list.mk。关键术语有 HDL/Verilog、FPGA、DSP、RF/LLRF、LBNL、GNU Make。核心结论有三：工具链地基为 Make + iverilog + Python；设计哲学是"命令行批处理优先、厂家图形工具并存"，厂家生成物统一放入 _<VENDOR_NAME>（如 _xilinx/）目录；dir_list.mk 通过 $(MAKEFILE_LIST) 自定位根目录并集中定义子目录绝对路径变量，使工程稳健可移植。完整顶层地图需把 README 子系统清单与 dir_list.mk 拼在一起看。
### u1-l2 构建与运行：工具链与 selftest

本讲解决在干净机器上跑起 Bedrock 的问题，聚焦两个最小模块：依赖清单 dependencies.txt 与测试编排脚本 selftest.sh。Bedrock 把工具分为必需（GNU Make、Icarus Verilog、python3-numpy、pip、flake8）与可选（Verilator、Yosys、scipy、matplotlib、cocotb 等）：numpy 被列为必需，因为多数校验脚本仅需其数组运算即可完成数值比对，而 scipy/matplotlib 仅在画图与高级信号处理中用到；这种分层降低了上手门槛。selftest.sh 是 GitLab CI test 阶段的本地翻版，本质是按字母顺序逐个子目录执行 make -C <dir>，外加环境版本巡检与 flake8 风格检查，建议用 sh -ex 运行使缺工具尽早暴露。核心结论：缺可选工具会让对应测试被 skip 而非整体失败；需要 migen、RISC-V 工具链或 SymbiYosys 的测试由 more 参数或注释门控；脚本故意不自动 git clean，clean 由各子目录 Makefile 负责，仅全程无错才打印 selftest OK。「make -C <dir> [target]」是后续各讲统一的手势。
### u1-l3 目录结构与代码导航

本讲讲授 Bedrock 这座大型 Verilog 库的目录组织与代码导航。dir_list.mk 是仓库「地址簿」，能自定位仓库根，为各子系统（如 dsp、cordic）定义绝对路径变量，使子目录可稳健互引。子目录 Makefile 遵循统一「标准骨架」——三段 include：公共的 top_rules.mk（方法与模式规则）和 bottom_rules.mk（clean）夹住本地 rules.mk（含 TEST_BENCH 测试台清单）。命名约定是认路关键：<模块>.v 为定义、<模块>_tb.v 为测试台、<模块>.gtkw 为波形配置，合称「三件套」。定位某模块用三步法：Grep/Glob 找定义、按约定找测试台并核对 TEST_BENCH、Grep 找实例化。关键反直觉：并非每模块都有独立测试台，如 mixer 仅被上层模块实例化而间接测试；命名约定只指明「去哪找」，不保证「一定存在」。
### u1-l4 RTL 编码规范

本讲是入门单元的收尾，讲解 Bedrock 的 RTL 编码规范，其唯一权威来源是 `guidelines/rtl_guidelines.md`，规则分为 A–E 五大类并逐条编号，便于代码评审引用。规范的核心目的只有一个：让人不用逆向工程，仅凭名字就能猜出模块的接口与用途。

A 类管**接口**——「接口」指一组协同完成某数据搬运协议的信号（如 localbus 的 `lb_clk/lb_addr/lb_rdata`），要求同一接口的信号共用前缀，且应优先复用 `valid/ready/wdata` 等标准命名而非自创。B 类管信号后缀：寄存/延迟用 `_r/_d`（多拍为 `_r1/_r2`），内部副本用 `_l/_i`，低有效用 `_n`，且全小写 snake_case。C 类要求参数全大写、自解释，且每个参数都有开箱即用的默认值（如 `DWI/AWI` 为合法位宽缩写）。D 类管模块实例化：实例名须以 `i_` 开头并含模块名，参数与端口一律按名连接，未用输出端口要显式留空并注释。E 类为杂项：仅允许 `TODO` 标签，注释须陈述事实不含问号，运算符优先级不显然时加括号。

本讲反复强调的关键判断力：**区分「规范要求」与「历史代码现状」**。Bedrock 是多年积累的库，dsp 子系统里位宽参数大量使用小写（如 `dwi`），延迟信号常省略 `_r`，这些属历史遗留偏离而非规范变更。典型样本为 `dsp/mixer.v`（同时含合规与偏离写法）、`cic_multichannel.v`（正面实例化）和 `cordic_wrap.v`（反面教材）。
### u2-l1 基于 Make 的 HDL 仿真测试方法

本讲是「核心方法学」首讲，讲解 Bedrock 如何用 GNU Make 驱动 iverilog 仿真。先对齐关键术语：Make 的「目标:依赖+命令」与自动变量 `$@`/`$<`/`$^`、iverilog 编译器、vvp 仿真运行器、testbench 测试台、VCD 波形文件、以及用 `%` 通配的模式规则。核心结论是：`top_rules.mk` 用一组共享「配方变量」的模式规则——`%_tb` 编译、`%_check` 仿真自校验、`%.vcd` 出波形、`%_view` 用 gtkwave 看波、`%_lint` 用 Verilator 静态检查——串成依赖流水线，敲一个 `make xxx_check` 即自动编译并仿真。两个进阶机制：`VFLAGS = ${VFLAGS_$@}` 钩子实现「按目标定制」（写 `VFLAGS_xxx_tb=...` 只影响单个 testbench）；iverilog 的 `-y`（按模块名搜目录）与 `-M`（输出用到的文件列表）配合生成 `.d` 依赖文件并按需 include，实现改子模块即自动重编。子目录通过「三段式 Makefile」（`dir_list.mk`→`top_rules.mk`→本地 `rules.mk`→`bottom_rules.mk`）接入公共骨架，本地只需填 `TEST_BENCH` 列表并用替换函数派生出 `checks`/`lint` 聚合目标。需注意：并非每个模块都有 `_tb`，如 `mixer` 无独立测试台。
### u2-l2 片上 localbus 总线

（讲义文件缺失，无摘要）
### u2-l3 寄存器地址映射自动生成 newad.py

本讲讲解寄存器地址映射自动生成工具 newad.py，核心是「单点标记、多点生成」：在 Verilog 端口写一行 magic 注释，工具即自动分配地址、生成解码器、地址表与寄存器表，样板代码降到接近零。两类 magic 注释：external 标记寄存器端口（可附 single-cycle/we-strobe/plus-we/strobe 属性），auto 标记需递归下钻的实例（可带时钟域覆盖与 generate 展开）；以 _addr 结尾的 output 会把同名 input 升级为 DPRAM 接口。newad 输入顶层 Verilog 与目录列表，产出 _auto.vh 解码器、addr_map_*.vh 地址命中宏、regmap_*.json 软件用名字-地址表；地址按位宽 2^n 对齐贪心分配，大块内存留空洞。parser.py 的 make_decoder_inner 按 signal_type 把同一端口展开为保持型寄存器、单脉冲、读选通、写使能或 DPRAM。默认正则后端轻量但脆弱，yosys 后端用真解析器规避局限，二者共享端口建模与地址逻辑；软件始终按名字访问寄存器。
### u3-l1 CORDIC 核：Python 生成的全展开流水线

本讲为 DSP 基础模块首讲，讲解 Bedrock 的 CORDIC 核——用移位与加减法完成旋转、求模、求角，并全展开成 nstg 级流水线的坐标旋转计算器。每级加减预定常数 arctan(2^-i)，固有增益约 1.64676 需调用方抵消；2 位 op 端口在旋转(P→R)、向量(R→P)、未用、follow 四种模式间切换且可逐拍变化。关键术语：DPW(数据通路位宽)、定点相位以自然二进制单位表示、模块不检测也不饱和溢出。实现上，Python 生成器 cordicgx.py 按 DPW 现场生成 cordicg_bN.v，atan 常数由 numpy 预算后烧成字面量、DPW 编进模块名；cstageg.v 为单级旋转单元，addsubg.v 为 control?a+b:a-b 原子构件。位宽由 Make 目标名 stem 决定而非 DPW 变量；width/nstg/def_op 是精度、延迟、资源的权衡旋钮。测试与校验分离：cordicg_tb.v 吐延迟对齐的输入输出对照表，cordic_check.py 用 numpy 黄金答案算 peak/rms 误差判 PASS/FAIL，默认 rms 0.36 bit 接近理论下限。CORDIC 作为三角函数引擎，是后续 rot_dds、mixer、fdownconvert 等模块的基础。
### u3-l2 混频器、DDS 与复数乘法

本讲（u3-l2）承接 u3-l1 的 CORDIC 核，讲解 Bedrock `dsp/` 子系统中最基础的 DDS 本振与混频数据通路。核心模块有四个：`ph_acc` 是相位累加器，用「20 位粗计数器 + 12 位细残差计数器 + 可编程模数」精确合成任意有理频率（如 SSRF 8/11、Argonne 9/13）的匀速相位，控制字刻意拆段以便在 32 位总线原子更新，输出取粗计数器高 19 位匹配 CORDIC。`rot_dds` 把 `ph_acc` 的相位喂给 CORDIC 旋转模式（`op=2'b00`），输出流水线化的 cos/sin 本振（LO），`lo_amp≈2^17/1.64676` 用以抵消 CORDIC 固有增益、防满量程溢出。`mixer` 是定点实数混频器（ADC×LO），难点在截位：输出位宽=dwi+davr，`davr` 直接调输出位宽，`NUM_DROP_BITS` 只平移截取窗口、用冗余符号位换低位精度而不改位宽；有 `NORMALIZE`（带四舍五入）与默认多级流水线两条分支。`complex_mul`（IQ 时分交织、2 乘法器、2 拍 1 结果、4 拍延迟）与 `complex_mul_flat`（IQ 并行、4 乘法器、1 拍 1 结果、3 拍延迟）是同一复乘 (a+jb)(c+jd) 的两种资源/吞吐权衡，均带 `SAT` 饱和与 `+1` 截断偏置校正。关键术语：DDS、本振 LO、混频、定点截位/饱和、IQ 交织、有理频率合成。本讲为下一讲 u3-l3 下变频/上变频搭好骨架。注意本仓库无独立 `mixer_tb`，`mixer` 经 `cic_multichannel_tb` 间接测试。
### u3-l3 数字下变频与上变频

本讲把 u3-l2 的单点乘法拼成完整射频数据通路：下变频 → 基带处理 → 上变频。`fdownconvert` 用相邻两 ADC 样本的矩阵运算一次性算出 (I,Q)，把「混频+低通」合并成隐式抽取滤波，输出**半速率交织 IQ**（一拍 I、一拍 Q 轮流），维持位宽守恒。`fiq_interp` 把交织半速率 IQ 解复用并做线性插值，还原成两条**全速率** I/Q 流（多 1 位增益）。`flevel_set` 是本振 (cos,sin) 与 (I,Q) 的**点积**，即上变频乘法核，含舍入、饱和与增益归一。`afterburner` 用割线系数 0.5·sec(φ) 求「圆上中点」，把速率翻倍喂 **DDR DAC**，输出偏移二进制。`ssb_out` 将上述积木拼成 **Hartley 单边带（SSB）调制器**：两次点积（第二次喂 90° 旋转 IQ）选边带，再升速到 DDR，最后翻 MSB 转回有符号。关键术语包括：IQ 基带、本振 LO、抽取/插值、交织 IQ 流、隐式抽取低通、点积、割线插值、偏移二进制、半速率 IQ；整条链路贯穿「**速率与位宽处处守恒**」的设计哲学，为下一讲正式滤波器与抽取插值铺垫。
### u3-l4 CIC、FIR/IIR 滤波器与抽取插值

本讲聚焦 Bedrock `dsp/` 子系统中的滤波与速率变换积木，前置承接 u3-l3 的射频数据通路。核心模块包括四类 CIC（无乘法器速率变换）：一阶抽取 `cic_simple_s`/`cic_simple_us`（抽取倍数由 `ex` 决定，寄存器宽 `dw+ex`，一阶差分延迟仅 1 个寄存器写死）、插值镜像 `cic_interp`、二阶多通道 `cic_multichannel`（积分器 `double_inte_smp` + 串行化 + 梳状 `ccfilt/doublediff`，差分深度由 `dsr_len`=`n_chan` 参数化）。关键位宽公式为增长位 = N·log₂R。`half_filt` 是 11 抽头无乘法器半带 FIR，用对称抽头 + 移位加减实现并自带抽取两倍。`biquad` 为二阶节 IIR 原子单元，单 MAC 分时复用做 5 次乘法，系数经 `sysClk` 写入、`reg_tech_cdc` 跨域到 `dataClk`，写地址 7 解除复位保证原子一致；`iirFilter` 用 one-hot `sysStageSelect` 串联多个 biquad 成高阶 IIR。共用存储原语 `fifo`/`circle_buf`/`dpram`/`reg_delay` 与一阶低通 `lpass1` 贯穿全流。本讲引出的 valid/ready 握手、CDC（`reg_tech_cdc`）等概念为 u4 时钟域跨越与 u6 射频控制链路作铺垫。
### u4-l1 时钟域跨越（CDC）基础

本讲讲时钟域跨越（CDC）基础。CDC 本质是处理异步时钟域间信号传递的亚稳态问题：单比特用两级同步器（MTBF 可达数千年以上），多位数据绝不能逐位打两拍（各比特跌落时刻不同会撕裂），须靠「源域稳定锁存 + 跨域 gate 资格认证」。

最小积木层层嵌套：`reg_tech_cdc` 是带 `ASYNC_REG` 与 `magic_cdc` 属性的同步器原子，也是 u6-l1 形式化检查工具 `cdc_snitch` 的锚点；`flag_xdomain` 用 toggle + 两级同步 + XOR 边沿检测把单比特事件脉冲搬到目的域，假设输入为稀疏脉冲（连发会被吞并告警）；`data_xdomain` 处理多位数据：源域在 `gate_in` 有效时锁存数据并保持，用 flag_xdomain 把 gate 跨过去，数据位仅取一级同步，亚稳态防护集中在 gate 上（前提是 clk_out 比 gate_in 速率快两倍以上）；`async_to_sync_reset_shift` 实现「异步复位、同步释放」的复位同步器，并把脉宽撑到 LENGTH 拍。

核心结论：localbus 写侧是单向跨域，用一行 `data_xdomain`（如 cryomodule.v 的 lb_to_1x，size=49=17 位地址+32 位数据）即可搬过时钟域，延迟可忽略；而读侧需请求-响应握手，localbus 刻意无握手，故读侧难——这正引出 u4-l2 存储网关（用固定延迟绕开握手）与 u4-l3 jit_rad（预警前把数据预快照入 dpram）存在的根本动机。
### u4-l2 存储网关与地址空间序列化

本讲承接 u4-l1「localbus 读侧难」的悬念，核心结论是：通过把读往返延迟在综合时固定为常量，让一个**无握手的 localbus** 能被 UDP 可靠读出。

讲义覆盖三个最小模块。其一为 **LASS（Lightweight Address Space Serialization，轻量地址空间序列化）协议**：它把对 24 位字地址、32 位数据的读/写串行化进 UDP 包，回包与请求包**结构、长度完全相同**，靠 64 位事务 ID 配对；事务分单拍（命令+地址 4 字节 + 数据 4 字节）与块传输两类，命令字节含一个读/写位和一个块传输位。

其二为 **mem_gateway**，即 localbus 主桥 client：下行用 `isr/c4/body` 把字节流拆成 localbus 周期，上行用一条 `read_pipe_len` 级的标记移位寄存器实现**固定延迟读**——读选通发出后恰好 `read_pipe_len` 拍（默认 3）触发 `capture` 采样 `data_in`，到点用读数据整体覆写输出移位寄存器 `osr`，填回回包占位段。关键参数 `read_pipe_len` 与 `n_lat`（约束 `n_lat ≥ 5 + read_pipe_len`）把延迟钉死，`finale` 用 `reg_delay` 补足到统一延迟。

其三为适配层：上游 **xformer** 按 `udp_sel` 把选通分发到最多 7 个 client 并重组回包（非零端口 UDP 校验和置零）；下游 **lb_gateway** 把 `control_*` 纯组合改名翻译成 `lb_*` 总线信号。大纲里的概念名 `LB_READ_DELAY` 即指由 `read_pipe_len` 实现的那个综合期常量。
### u4-l3 jit_rad：跨域即时读回

本讲承接 u4-l2，解决无握手 localbus 读侧跨域难的问题。核心结论：localbus 读侧是「请求—响应」往返，无法约定数据何时就绪，而 mem_gateway 又要求综合期固定延迟，导致跨域读不可行；常见坏实践是「直接无视 CDC 赌寄存器不被改坏」。jit_rad 的策略是不在读发生时跨域，而利用 LASS UDP 包到达前 300~352 ns 的预警窗口（warning window），让 app_clk 域跑约 17 拍，把 16 个字经 app 侧多路选择器预先搬进一块 `16×32` 双端口 `dpram`（A 口 app_clk 写、B 口 lb_clk 读），随后 localbus 同域读回，CDC 问题消失。可行性等价于时序不等式 `T_warn > 16·T_app + T_sync`。关键术语：主模块 `jit_rad_gateway`（lb 侧与 xfer/app 侧两组接口）、`lb_prefill`（触发搬移，经 `flag_xdomain` 跨域成 app_prefill/xfer_snap）、`xfer_snap`（原子快照钩子）、`lb_error`（守护时序违约）、`passthrough` 参数（=0 缓冲模式 CDC 正确，=1 直通复刻坏 CDC，会被 cdc_snitch 判违规）。语义边界：同一包内重复读必得相同值（快照静态、默认非原子），可用 `xfer_snap` 实现多寄存器一致快照。
### u4-l4 Packet Badger：以太网/IP/UDP 响应核

（讲义文件缺失，无摘要）
### u5-l1 serial_io：8b/10b、GMII 与链路层

---

## 本讲义规格

- id: u6-l1
- 文件名: u6-l1-cdc-snitch-verification.md
- 写入路径: Bedrock-tutorial/u6-l1-cdc-snitch-verification.md
- 标题: cdc_snitch：形式化跨域验证
- 学习阶段: advanced
- 主题: 讲解 build-tools/cdc_snitch：用 yosys 把设计读入并标注 magic_cdc 属性，自动检测违反 CDC 规则的跨域路径，CI 中用于保证 jit_rad 等设计的正确性。
- 学习目标: 理解 cdc_snitch 用 yosys JSON 输出做静态 CDC 分析的原理；掌握 magic_cdc 属性、cdc_snitch_proc.ys 流程脚本的作用；明白为何 passthrough=1 会被 cdc_snitch 判为违规（与 jit_rad 对照）
- 应覆盖的最小模块: cdc_snitch.py 分析器，cdc_snitch_proc.ys yosys 流程，%_cdc.txt 模式规则
- 关键源码: build-tools/cdc_snitch.md，build-tools/cdc_snitch.py，build-tools/cdc_snitch_proc.ys，build-tools/top_rules.mk
- 代码实践任务: 阅读 cdc_snitch.md，运行 localbus 目录的 CDC 检查（`make -C localbus` 中相关 _cdc.txt 目标，若可用），解读输出中 OK/违规路径的含义；指出 cdc_BAD.svg 与 cdc_OK1.svg 的区别。
- 依赖讲义: u4-l1

---

## 任务

要求：

1. 只生成 `Bedrock-tutorial/u6-l1-cdc-snitch-verification.md` 这一个文件。
2. 必须结合真实源码。
3. 必须包含代码实践。
4. 必须使用永久链接引用源码。
5. new/rebuild 模式下从零写入。
6. update 模式下先读取旧文件，再结合 diff 更新。
7. 不要修改源码。
8. 不要写其他文件。

完成后，用一句话总结本讲义覆盖的最小模块。

当前 HEAD: 235f3e3b5602790927caf62a405fce81213bb3de
动作: new


---

## 项目学习路线（大纲方法论）

# 项目学习手册·大纲规划 Planner

## 角色

你是资深技术教育专家、源码阅读导师和项目学习路线设计者。

你运行在被分析项目的根目录内。

你可以使用：

- `Read`
- `Grep`
- `Glob`
- 只读 `git` 命令，例如 `git log`、`git diff`、`git show`、`git ls-files`

你不能：

- 修改源码
- 写入任何 `.md` 文件
- 输出 JSON 以外的文字

你的唯一产出是一个学习手册大纲 manifest JSON。

---

## 总目标

为当前项目规划一套「从零开始、由浅入深、结合源码和代码实践」的学习手册。

这套手册会被拆分成多篇 Markdown 讲义，后续 worker 会根据 manifest 逐篇生成。

每篇讲义应该满足：

1. 面向初学者也能读懂。
2. 只讲一个相对独立的主题。
3. 必须结合真实源码。
4. 必须包含一个可操作的代码实践任务。
5. 讲义之间有清晰依赖关系。

---

## 源码探查顺序

请按以下顺序理解项目，不要一开始就深入细节：

1. 项目说明
   - README
   - docs
   - examples
   - tutorials

2. 构建与运行方式
   - package.json
   - Cargo.toml
   - pyproject.toml
   - go.mod
   - pom.xml
   - Makefile
   - Dockerfile
   - CI 配置

3. 项目入口
   - CLI 入口
   - Web 服务入口
   - main 文件
   - library export 文件
   - examples 入口

4. 目录结构
   - src
   - lib
   - app
   - packages
   - crates
   - tests

5. 核心流程
   - 初始化流程
   - 配置加载
   - 请求/任务/数据处理主链路
   - 插件、扩展、调度、存储等核心机制

6. 测试与示例
   - 单元测试
   - 集成测试
   - benchmark
   - example usage

---

## 学习路线设计原则

请把学习手册设计成三层：

1. 入门层 beginner
   目标：让读者知道项目是什么、怎么运行、目录如何组织、核心入口在哪里。

2. 进阶层 intermediate
   目标：让读者理解主要模块、核心调用链、关键数据结构和配置机制。

3. 专家层 advanced
   目标：让读者理解扩展点、性能、并发、错误处理、测试、二次开发和架构取舍。

---

## 讲义拆分规则

- 每篇讲义只解决一个主要问题。
- 每篇讲义包含 2 到 5 个最小模块。
- 每篇讲义必须有一个 `practice_task`，描述读者要完成的代码实践。
- 每篇讲义的源码范围要适中，通常引用 1 到 6 个关键源码文件。
- 大型项目可以拆成 30 篇以上讲义；中型项目 15 到 30 篇；小型项目 8 到 15 篇。
- 不要为了数量硬拆，逻辑优先。
- 第一单元必须帮助读者从零开始，包括项目定位、运行方式、目录结构、入口文件。
- 后续单元再进入核心模块、源码机制、扩展实践。

---

## mode=full

首次生成大纲时：

1. 阅读项目关键文件。
2. 理解项目定位、技术栈、运行方式和核心模块。
3. 设计从零开始的学习路径。
4. 输出完整 manifest。
5. 所有讲义的 `action` 都必须是 `"new"`。

---

## mode=incremental

增量更新时：

1. 读取 stdin 中提供的现有 manifest。
2. 阅读项目中已有的 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 讲义目录，理解旧结构。
3. 使用：

   - `git diff previous_head..current_head`
   - `git log`
   - `git show`

   分析代码变化。

4. 输出更新后的完整 manifest。

每篇讲义的 `action` 按以下规则设置：

- `"keep"`
  代码变化不影响这篇讲义，无需重写。

- `"update"`
  小范围改动，例如函数重命名、参数调整、局部逻辑变化、链接行号变化。

- `"new"`
  新增功能、新增模块、新增重要机制，需要新增讲义。

- `"rebuild"`
  架构、主流程、公共接口、核心模型发生重大变化，旧讲义需要重建。

如果某篇旧讲义已经不再适合当前项目结构，可以从 manifest 中移除。

---

## manifest 输出格式

只输出严格 JSON，不要输出 Markdown，不要输出解释文字。

```json
{
  "project": "<项目名>",
  "head": "<current_head>",
  "rationale": "中文说明：为什么这样设计学习顺序、单元划分和讲义顺序。",
  "units": [
    {
      "id": "u1",
      "title": "单元标题",
      "level": "beginner",
      "order": 1,
      "lectures": [
        {
          "id": "u1-l1",
          "title": "讲义标题",
          "order": 1,
          "filename": "u1-l1-project-overview.md",
          "level": "beginner",
          "topic": "本讲义要讲什么，用 1 到 3 句中文说明。",
          "learning_goals": [
            "学习目标 1",
            "学习目标 2"
          ],
          "source_files": [
            "README.md",
            "src/main.ts"
          ],
          "minimal_modules": [
            "模块 1",
            "模块 2"
          ],
          "practice_task": "本讲义对应的代码实践任务。",
          "depends_on": [],
          "action": "new"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## 字段要求

- `project`：项目名。
- `head`：当前 git HEAD。
- `rationale`：中文，一段即可。
- `unit.id`：格式为 `u1`、`u2`、`u3`。
- `lecture.id`：格式为 `u1-l1`、`u1-l2`。
- `filename`：
  - 必须全局唯一。
  - 必须小写。
  - 使用连字符。
  - 必须以 `.md` 结尾。
  - 文件位于 `<项目名>-tutorial/` 目录下。
- `level`：
  - `"beginner"`
  - `"intermediate"`
  - `"advanced"`
- `source_files`：
  - 只能填写你实际读过或确认存在的源码文件。
  - 不要编造文件路径。
- `depends_on`：
  - 使用讲义 id。
  - 没有依赖时填写空数组。
- `action`：
  - full 模式全部为 `"new"`。
  - incremental 模式使用 `"keep"`、`"update"`、`"new"`、`"rebuild"`。

---

## 重要约束

- 只基于真实源码和项目文件规划。
- 不确定的地方写 `"待确认"`。
- 不要编造不存在的模块、命令、接口或源码文件。
- 输出必须是合法 JSON。

## 前置讲义摘要（你必须基于这些已建立的认知继续，不要重复，要承接）

### u1-l1 项目总览：Bedrock 是什么

本讲是 Bedrock 学习手册的开篇，回答三个问题：Bedrock 是什么、由哪些部分组成、从哪里看起。结论是：Bedrock 是 LBNL 多年积累的、平台无关的 Verilog 代码库，用于 DSP 与 RF（含 LLRF）控制，并附带到 FPGA（如 Xilinx）所需的全部适配层。全讲只涉及仓库根目录两个最小模块：README.md 的顶层说明，以及 dir_list.mk 的目录常量定义。Bedrock 是一个多子系统聚合体，主要子系统包括 dsp（数字信号处理算法）、cordic（坐标旋转计算）、rtsim（RF 系统实时仿真）、cmoc（RF 控制器）、badger（响应 Ethernet/IP/UDP 的硬件核）、localbus（片上总线）、fpga_family 与 board_support（厂家与板卡适配）、projects（具体上板工程）等；soc/picorv32（RISC-V 软核 SoC）、serial_io、peripheral_drivers、build-tools 等仅见于 dir_list.mk。关键术语有 HDL/Verilog、FPGA、DSP、RF/LLRF、LBNL、GNU Make。核心结论有三：工具链地基为 Make + iverilog + Python；设计哲学是"命令行批处理优先、厂家图形工具并存"，厂家生成物统一放入 _<VENDOR_NAME>（如 _xilinx/）目录；dir_list.mk 通过 $(MAKEFILE_LIST) 自定位根目录并集中定义子目录绝对路径变量，使工程稳健可移植。完整顶层地图需把 README 子系统清单与 dir_list.mk 拼在一起看。
### u1-l2 构建与运行：工具链与 selftest

本讲解决在干净机器上跑起 Bedrock 的问题，聚焦两个最小模块：依赖清单 dependencies.txt 与测试编排脚本 selftest.sh。Bedrock 把工具分为必需（GNU Make、Icarus Verilog、python3-numpy、pip、flake8）与可选（Verilator、Yosys、scipy、matplotlib、cocotb 等）：numpy 被列为必需，因为多数校验脚本仅需其数组运算即可完成数值比对，而 scipy/matplotlib 仅在画图与高级信号处理中用到；这种分层降低了上手门槛。selftest.sh 是 GitLab CI test 阶段的本地翻版，本质是按字母顺序逐个子目录执行 make -C <dir>，外加环境版本巡检与 flake8 风格检查，建议用 sh -ex 运行使缺工具尽早暴露。核心结论：缺可选工具会让对应测试被 skip 而非整体失败；需要 migen、RISC-V 工具链或 SymbiYosys 的测试由 more 参数或注释门控；脚本故意不自动 git clean，clean 由各子目录 Makefile 负责，仅全程无错才打印 selftest OK。「make -C <dir> [target]」是后续各讲统一的手势。
### u1-l3 目录结构与代码导航

本讲讲授 Bedrock 这座大型 Verilog 库的目录组织与代码导航。dir_list.mk 是仓库「地址簿」，能自定位仓库根，为各子系统（如 dsp、cordic）定义绝对路径变量，使子目录可稳健互引。子目录 Makefile 遵循统一「标准骨架」——三段 include：公共的 top_rules.mk（方法与模式规则）和 bottom_rules.mk（clean）夹住本地 rules.mk（含 TEST_BENCH 测试台清单）。命名约定是认路关键：<模块>.v 为定义、<模块>_tb.v 为测试台、<模块>.gtkw 为波形配置，合称「三件套」。定位某模块用三步法：Grep/Glob 找定义、按约定找测试台并核对 TEST_BENCH、Grep 找实例化。关键反直觉：并非每模块都有独立测试台，如 mixer 仅被上层模块实例化而间接测试；命名约定只指明「去哪找」，不保证「一定存在」。
### u1-l4 RTL 编码规范

本讲是入门单元的收尾，讲解 Bedrock 的 RTL 编码规范，其唯一权威来源是 `guidelines/rtl_guidelines.md`，规则分为 A–E 五大类并逐条编号，便于代码评审引用。规范的核心目的只有一个：让人不用逆向工程，仅凭名字就能猜出模块的接口与用途。

A 类管**接口**——「接口」指一组协同完成某数据搬运协议的信号（如 localbus 的 `lb_clk/lb_addr/lb_rdata`），要求同一接口的信号共用前缀，且应优先复用 `valid/ready/wdata` 等标准命名而非自创。B 类管信号后缀：寄存/延迟用 `_r/_d`（多拍为 `_r1/_r2`），内部副本用 `_l/_i`，低有效用 `_n`，且全小写 snake_case。C 类要求参数全大写、自解释，且每个参数都有开箱即用的默认值（如 `DWI/AWI` 为合法位宽缩写）。D 类管模块实例化：实例名须以 `i_` 开头并含模块名，参数与端口一律按名连接，未用输出端口要显式留空并注释。E 类为杂项：仅允许 `TODO` 标签，注释须陈述事实不含问号，运算符优先级不显然时加括号。

本讲反复强调的关键判断力：**区分「规范要求」与「历史代码现状」**。Bedrock 是多年积累的库，dsp 子系统里位宽参数大量使用小写（如 `dwi`），延迟信号常省略 `_r`，这些属历史遗留偏离而非规范变更。典型样本为 `dsp/mixer.v`（同时含合规与偏离写法）、`cic_multichannel.v`（正面实例化）和 `cordic_wrap.v`（反面教材）。
### u2-l1 基于 Make 的 HDL 仿真测试方法

本讲是「核心方法学」首讲，讲解 Bedrock 如何用 GNU Make 驱动 iverilog 仿真。先对齐关键术语：Make 的「目标:依赖+命令」与自动变量 `$@`/`$<`/`$^`、iverilog 编译器、vvp 仿真运行器、testbench 测试台、VCD 波形文件、以及用 `%` 通配的模式规则。核心结论是：`top_rules.mk` 用一组共享「配方变量」的模式规则——`%_tb` 编译、`%_check` 仿真自校验、`%.vcd` 出波形、`%_view` 用 gtkwave 看波、`%_lint` 用 Verilator 静态检查——串成依赖流水线，敲一个 `make xxx_check` 即自动编译并仿真。两个进阶机制：`VFLAGS = ${VFLAGS_$@}` 钩子实现「按目标定制」（写 `VFLAGS_xxx_tb=...` 只影响单个 testbench）；iverilog 的 `-y`（按模块名搜目录）与 `-M`（输出用到的文件列表）配合生成 `.d` 依赖文件并按需 include，实现改子模块即自动重编。子目录通过「三段式 Makefile」（`dir_list.mk`→`top_rules.mk`→本地 `rules.mk`→`bottom_rules.mk`）接入公共骨架，本地只需填 `TEST_BENCH` 列表并用替换函数派生出 `checks`/`lint` 聚合目标。需注意：并非每个模块都有 `_tb`，如 `mixer` 无独立测试台。
### u2-l2 片上 localbus 总线

### u2-l3 寄存器地址映射自动生成 newad.py

本讲讲解寄存器地址映射自动生成工具 newad.py，核心是「单点标记、多点生成」：在 Verilog 端口写一行 magic 注释，工具即自动分配地址、生成解码器、地址表与寄存器表，样板代码降到接近零。两类 magic 注释：external 标记寄存器端口（可附 single-cycle/we-strobe/plus-we/strobe 属性），auto 标记需递归下钻的实例（可带时钟域覆盖与 generate 展开）；以 _addr 结尾的 output 会把同名 input 升级为 DPRAM 接口。newad 输入顶层 Verilog 与目录列表，产出 _auto.vh 解码器、addr_map_*.vh 地址命中宏、regmap_*.json 软件用名字-地址表；地址按位宽 2^n 对齐贪心分配，大块内存留空洞。parser.py 的 make_decoder_inner 按 signal_type 把同一端口展开为保持型寄存器、单脉冲、读选通、写使能或 DPRAM。默认正则后端轻量但脆弱，yosys 后端用真解析器规避局限，二者共享端口建模与地址逻辑；软件始终按名字访问寄存器。
### u3-l1 CORDIC 核：Python 生成的全展开流水线

本讲为 DSP 基础模块首讲，讲解 Bedrock 的 CORDIC 核——用移位与加减法完成旋转、求模、求角，并全展开成 nstg 级流水线的坐标旋转计算器。每级加减预定常数 arctan(2^-i)，固有增益约 1.64676 需调用方抵消；2 位 op 端口在旋转(P→R)、向量(R→P)、未用、follow 四种模式间切换且可逐拍变化。关键术语：DPW(数据通路位宽)、定点相位以自然二进制单位表示、模块不检测也不饱和溢出。实现上，Python 生成器 cordicgx.py 按 DPW 现场生成 cordicg_bN.v，atan 常数由 numpy 预算后烧成字面量、DPW 编进模块名；cstageg.v 为单级旋转单元，addsubg.v 为 control?a+b:a-b 原子构件。位宽由 Make 目标名 stem 决定而非 DPW 变量；width/nstg/def_op 是精度、延迟、资源的权衡旋钮。测试与校验分离：cordicg_tb.v 吐延迟对齐的输入输出对照表，cordic_check.py 用 numpy 黄金答案算 peak/rms 误差判 PASS/FAIL，默认 rms 0.36 bit 接近理论下限。CORDIC 作为三角函数引擎，是后续 rot_dds、mixer、fdownconvert 等模块的基础。
### u3-l2 混频器、DDS 与复数乘法

本讲（u3-l2）承接 u3-l1 的 CORDIC 核，讲解 Bedrock `dsp/` 子系统中最基础的 DDS 本振与混频数据通路。核心模块有四个：`ph_acc` 是相位累加器，用「20 位粗计数器 + 12 位细残差计数器 + 可编程模数」精确合成任意有理频率（如 SSRF 8/11、Argonne 9/13）的匀速相位，控制字刻意拆段以便在 32 位总线原子更新，输出取粗计数器高 19 位匹配 CORDIC。`rot_dds` 把 `ph_acc` 的相位喂给 CORDIC 旋转模式（`op=2'b00`），输出流水线化的 cos/sin 本振（LO），`lo_amp≈2^17/1.64676` 用以抵消 CORDIC 固有增益、防满量程溢出。`mixer` 是定点实数混频器（ADC×LO），难点在截位：输出位宽=dwi+davr，`davr` 直接调输出位宽，`NUM_DROP_BITS` 只平移截取窗口、用冗余符号位换低位精度而不改位宽；有 `NORMALIZE`（带四舍五入）与默认多级流水线两条分支。`complex_mul`（IQ 时分交织、2 乘法器、2 拍 1 结果、4 拍延迟）与 `complex_mul_flat`（IQ 并行、4 乘法器、1 拍 1 结果、3 拍延迟）是同一复乘 (a+jb)(c+jd) 的两种资源/吞吐权衡，均带 `SAT` 饱和与 `+1` 截断偏置校正。关键术语：DDS、本振 LO、混频、定点截位/饱和、IQ 交织、有理频率合成。本讲为下一讲 u3-l3 下变频/上变频搭好骨架。注意本仓库无独立 `mixer_tb`，`mixer` 经 `cic_multichannel_tb` 间接测试。
### u3-l3 数字下变频与上变频

本讲把 u3-l2 的单点乘法拼成完整射频数据通路：下变频 → 基带处理 → 上变频。`fdownconvert` 用相邻两 ADC 样本的矩阵运算一次性算出 (I,Q)，把「混频+低通」合并成隐式抽取滤波，输出**半速率交织 IQ**（一拍 I、一拍 Q 轮流），维持位宽守恒。`fiq_interp` 把交织半速率 IQ 解复用并做线性插值，还原成两条**全速率** I/Q 流（多 1 位增益）。`flevel_set` 是本振 (cos,sin) 与 (I,Q) 的**点积**，即上变频乘法核，含舍入、饱和与增益归一。`afterburner` 用割线系数 0.5·sec(φ) 求「圆上中点」，把速率翻倍喂 **DDR DAC**，输出偏移二进制。`ssb_out` 将上述积木拼成 **Hartley 单边带（SSB）调制器**：两次点积（第二次喂 90° 旋转 IQ）选边带，再升速到 DDR，最后翻 MSB 转回有符号。关键术语包括：IQ 基带、本振 LO、抽取/插值、交织 IQ 流、隐式抽取低通、点积、割线插值、偏移二进制、半速率 IQ；整条链路贯穿「**速率与位宽处处守恒**」的设计哲学，为下一讲正式滤波器与抽取插值铺垫。
### u3-l4 CIC、FIR/IIR 滤波器与抽取插值

本讲聚焦 Bedrock `dsp/` 子系统中的滤波与速率变换积木，前置承接 u3-l3 的射频数据通路。核心模块包括四类 CIC（无乘法器速率变换）：一阶抽取 `cic_simple_s`/`cic_simple_us`（抽取倍数由 `ex` 决定，寄存器宽 `dw+ex`，一阶差分延迟仅 1 个寄存器写死）、插值镜像 `cic_interp`、二阶多通道 `cic_multichannel`（积分器 `double_inte_smp` + 串行化 + 梳状 `ccfilt/doublediff`，差分深度由 `dsr_len`=`n_chan` 参数化）。关键位宽公式为增长位 = N·log₂R。`half_filt` 是 11 抽头无乘法器半带 FIR，用对称抽头 + 移位加减实现并自带抽取两倍。`biquad` 为二阶节 IIR 原子单元，单 MAC 分时复用做 5 次乘法，系数经 `sysClk` 写入、`reg_tech_cdc` 跨域到 `dataClk`，写地址 7 解除复位保证原子一致；`iirFilter` 用 one-hot `sysStageSelect` 串联多个 biquad 成高阶 IIR。共用存储原语 `fifo`/`circle_buf`/`dpram`/`reg_delay` 与一阶低通 `lpass1` 贯穿全流。本讲引出的 valid/ready 握手、CDC（`reg_tech_cdc`）等概念为 u4 时钟域跨越与 u6 射频控制链路作铺垫。
### u4-l1 时钟域跨越（CDC）基础

本讲讲时钟域跨越（CDC）基础。CDC 本质是处理异步时钟域间信号传递的亚稳态问题：单比特用两级同步器（MTBF 可达数千年以上），多位数据绝不能逐位打两拍（各比特跌落时刻不同会撕裂），须靠「源域稳定锁存 + 跨域 gate 资格认证」。

最小积木层层嵌套：`reg_tech_cdc` 是带 `ASYNC_REG` 与 `magic_cdc` 属性的同步器原子，也是 u6-l1 形式化检查工具 `cdc_snitch` 的锚点；`flag_xdomain` 用 toggle + 两级同步 + XOR 边沿检测把单比特事件脉冲搬到目的域，假设输入为稀疏脉冲（连发会被吞并告警）；`data_xdomain` 处理多位数据：源域在 `gate_in` 有效时锁存数据并保持，用 flag_xdomain 把 gate 跨过去，数据位仅取一级同步，亚稳态防护集中在 gate 上（前提是 clk_out 比 gate_in 速率快两倍以上）；`async_to_sync_reset_shift` 实现「异步复位、同步释放」的复位同步器，并把脉宽撑到 LENGTH 拍。

核心结论：localbus 写侧是单向跨域，用一行 `data_xdomain`（如 cryomodule.v 的 lb_to_1x，size=49=17 位地址+32 位数据）即可搬过时钟域，延迟可忽略；而读侧需请求-响应握手，localbus 刻意无握手，故读侧难——这正引出 u4-l2 存储网关（用固定延迟绕开握手）与 u4-l3 jit_rad（预警前把数据预快照入 dpram）存在的根本动机。
### u4-l2 存储网关与地址空间序列化

本讲承接 u4-l1「localbus 读侧难」的悬念，核心结论是：通过把读往返延迟在综合时固定为常量，让一个**无握手的 localbus** 能被 UDP 可靠读出。

讲义覆盖三个最小模块。其一为 **LASS（Lightweight Address Space Serialization，轻量地址空间序列化）协议**：它把对 24 位字地址、32 位数据的读/写串行化进 UDP 包，回包与请求包**结构、长度完全相同**，靠 64 位事务 ID 配对；事务分单拍（命令+地址 4 字节 + 数据 4 字节）与块传输两类，命令字节含一个读/写位和一个块传输位。

其二为 **mem_gateway**，即 localbus 主桥 client：下行用 `isr/c4/body` 把字节流拆成 localbus 周期，上行用一条 `read_pipe_len` 级的标记移位寄存器实现**固定延迟读**——读选通发出后恰好 `read_pipe_len` 拍（默认 3）触发 `capture` 采样 `data_in`，到点用读数据整体覆写输出移位寄存器 `osr`，填回回包占位段。关键参数 `read_pipe_len` 与 `n_lat`（约束 `n_lat ≥ 5 + read_pipe_len`）把延迟钉死，`finale` 用 `reg_delay` 补足到统一延迟。

其三为适配层：上游 **xformer** 按 `udp_sel` 把选通分发到最多 7 个 client 并重组回包（非零端口 UDP 校验和置零）；下游 **lb_gateway** 把 `control_*` 纯组合改名翻译成 `lb_*` 总线信号。大纲里的概念名 `LB_READ_DELAY` 即指由 `read_pipe_len` 实现的那个综合期常量。
### u4-l3 jit_rad：跨域即时读回

本讲承接 u4-l2，解决无握手 localbus 读侧跨域难的问题。核心结论：localbus 读侧是「请求—响应」往返，无法约定数据何时就绪，而 mem_gateway 又要求综合期固定延迟，导致跨域读不可行；常见坏实践是「直接无视 CDC 赌寄存器不被改坏」。jit_rad 的策略是不在读发生时跨域，而利用 LASS UDP 包到达前 300~352 ns 的预警窗口（warning window），让 app_clk 域跑约 17 拍，把 16 个字经 app 侧多路选择器预先搬进一块 `16×32` 双端口 `dpram`（A 口 app_clk 写、B 口 lb_clk 读），随后 localbus 同域读回，CDC 问题消失。可行性等价于时序不等式 `T_warn > 16·T_app + T_sync`。关键术语：主模块 `jit_rad_gateway`（lb 侧与 xfer/app 侧两组接口）、`lb_prefill`（触发搬移，经 `flag_xdomain` 跨域成 app_prefill/xfer_snap）、`xfer_snap`（原子快照钩子）、`lb_error`（守护时序违约）、`passthrough` 参数（=0 缓冲模式 CDC 正确，=1 直通复刻坏 CDC，会被 cdc_snitch 判违规）。语义边界：同一包内重复读必得相同值（快照静态、默认非原子），可用 `xfer_snap` 实现多寄存器一致快照。

5. new/rebuild 模式下从零写入。
6. update 模式下先读取旧文件，再结合 diff 更新。
7. 不要修改源码。
8. 不要写其他文件。

完成后，用一句话总结本讲义覆盖的最小模块。

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	# cdc\_snitch Documentation
2	
3	`cdc_snitch.py` is a python script which, combined with
4	[yosys](https://yosyshq.net/yosys/) processing of Verilog files,
5	can detect anti-patterns of Clock-Domain-Crossing (CDC).
6	
7	We use yosys to synthesize and break down the design to registers,
8	combinational logic, and memories.  Using json as an intermediate
9	file format, `cdc_snitch.py` categorizes each register by how its
10	clock domain does or doesn't match the clock domains of its data sources.
11	
12	## OK1
13	
14	![OK1 topology](cdc_OK1.svg)
15	
16	If all inputs leading to a register are in the same domain as its own
17	clock, that's called "OK1".  This should be the standard topology for all
18	real computation and state-machines.
19	
20	## OKX or CDC
21	
22	![OKX topology](cdc_OKX.svg)
23	
24	If each input comes from a single register in another domain, so the
25	combinational logic is a trivial 'straight wire', that's called "OKX" or "CDC".
26	All real CDC circuits should be built with this topology at their core.
27	The default category is "OKX"; if the register has a magic CDC attribute,
28	then it gets labeled "CDC".  That attribute is intended to mark
29	_intentional_ CDC crossings.  `cdc_snitch` itself has no way to know
30	if such intentional CDC crossings are properly designed to avoid
31	data corruption.
32	
33	## BAD
34	
35	![BAD topology](cdc_BAD.svg)
36	
37	Registers with combinational input, that get at least one input not from
38	its own domain, are categorized as "BAD".
39	Maybe this rule could be relaxed some for an ASIC, but in an FPGA
40	there are no expectations that the output of a LUT will be glitch-free
41	when inputs change.
42	
43	Designs with a non-zero number of BAD registers cause `cdc_snitch.py` to
44	return with an error, as is relevant for a regression-testing makefile.
45	
46	## Memories
47	
48	Memories are a special case.  If used to cross clock domains,
49	the general case would necessarily mark them as "BAD" because
50	the inputs to the output register include the memory cells
51	(in the input domain) and address bits (in the output domain).
52	But when used properly, a data word is not written to and read from
53	at the same time.
54	So `cdc_snitch` ignores what goes on inside dual-port memories.
55	
56	## I/O
57	
58	Each top-level input port is placed in its own domain,
59	and output ports are not checked for errors.
60	This may or may not represent the demands of a specific use case.
61	We have written shells around the logic-under-test, that add
62	input and output registers to define the domain of each I/O signal.
63	If an input _does_ represent a physical pin, the own-domain rule
64	will give reasonable results.  Such a design will normally capture
65	the input in a simple register (placed by the synthesizer in the I/O cell),
66	and that's a valid "OKX" or (with attribute) "CDC".
67	
68	## Verilog input
69	
70	Because we call on yosys to process the source Verilog, that Verilog
71	needs to be portable and synthesizable, and not instantiate (closed-source,
72	non-synthesizable) vendor primitives.  For many reasons, we try to layer
73	our designs to put such constructions in an outer chip-specific layer,
74	with the Real Work instantiated as a portable Verilog module on the inside.
75	You would then point `cdc_snitch` at that inner layer.
76	
77	Of course, `cdc_snitch` itself doesn't know or care what language
78	you feed into yosys.  Verilog and a limited subset of SystemVerilog are OK with yosys-0.23.
79	
80	## Tool flow
81	
82	Our makefiles specify rules based on the following
83	```
84	foo_yosys.json: foo_shell.v foo.v worker1.v worker2.v $(BUILD_DIR)/cdc_snitch_proc.ys
85		$(YOSYS) -p "read_verilog  $(filter %.v, $^); script $(filter %_proc.ys, $^); write_json $@"
86	foo_cdc.txt: $(BUILD_DIR)/cdc_snitch.py foo_yosys.json
87		$(PYTHON) $^ -o $@
88	```
89	The chatter from that last command ends with a line like
90	```
91	OK1: 81504  CDC: 337  OKX: 1105  BAD: 97
92	```
93	counting the number of registers in each category.
94	The `foo_cdc.txt` file gives details, which ideally can help you find the
95	design errors that led to the "BAD" registers.
96	If the design includes any such "BAD" registers, the makefile rule for
97	`foo_cdc.txt` will fail, as would be used for a regression test.
98	
99	## Reading the output
100	
101	It's recommended to save the output of `cdc_snitch.py` in a file by
102	using the `-o` flag.
103	Call the result `foo_cdc.txt`, consistent with the make rule above.
104	That will usually be a big file!  But it is text, and can be understood
105	as described here.
106	
107	Ideally, the BAD count is zero, and you don't need to look at `foo_cdc.txt`.
108	Otherwise, the best way to start is with
109	```
110	grep BAD foo_cdc.txt
111	```
112	Giving one line per DFF that falls in the BAD category described above.
113	Each of those lines will look something like
114	```
115	BAD  31049 dsp.reg_bank_2[0]:D clk lb_clk inputs ( 8 x lb_clk, 3 x dsp_clk, 1 x dsp.evr_rx_out_clk )
116	```
117	Here `dsp.reg_bank_2[0]:D` tells you that the offending path is the D input
118	to the 0 element of word full of DFF named (by yosys) as `dsp.reg_bank_2`.
119	Each net often has many different possible names, but you should be able to
120	find the `reg_bank_2` signal in instance `dsp` in your code.
121	
122	Then it says `clk lb_clk` identifying the clock to the DFF.
123	Finally `inputs ( 8 x lb_clk, 3 x dsp_clk, 1 x dsp.evr_rx_out_clk )`
124	is a count of signals per clock domain feeding the logic.
125	The goal is to reduce these inputs to a single clock domain,
126	as described in case OK1 above.
127	
128	To dive deeper, using that same example, read the whole `foo_cdc.txt`
129	file with `less` or your favorite text editor.  Now search for the
130	line discovered above with `grep`.  It is followed by more information
131	about that BAD DFF input.
132	```
133	BAD  31049 dsp.reg_bank_2[0]:D clk lb_clk inputs ( 8 x lb_clk, 3 x dsp_clk, 1 x dsp.evr_rx_out_clk )
134	  tree 31049 from 397 clk lb_clk name lb_addr_r[0]
135	  tree 31049 from 398 clk lb_clk name lb_addr_r[1]
136	  tree 31049 from 399 clk lb_clk name lb_addr_r[2]
137	  tree 31049 from 400 clk lb_clk name lb_addr_r[3]
138	  tree 31049 from 18908 clk lb_clk name dsp.fcnt_dsp_clk.work.frequency[0]
139	  tree 31049 from 28493 clk lb_clk name dsp.timing.evr_timestamp_valid
140	  tree 31049 from 28547 clk dsp_clk name dsp.evr_live_pps_tick[0]
141	  tree 31049 from 29201 clk dsp_clk name dsp.timing.i_oc_sync.data_out[0]
142	  tree 31049 from 29265 clk dsp_clk name dsp.timing.i_oc_sync.data_out[32]
143	  tree 31049 from 29529 clk dsp.evr_rx_out_clk name dsp.timing.i_evrAROC.evrSROCsynced
144	  tree 31049 from 29536 clk lb_clk name dsp.timing.i_evcnt_sync.data_out[0]
145	  tree 31049 from 29724 clk lb_clk name dsp.timing.fcnt_evr_clk.work.frequency[0]
146	```
147	Now instead of just a count, you can see each input to the logic cloud
148	identified by name and clock domain.  So `lb_addr_r[0]` comes from the
149	lb\_clk domain, and `dsp.evr_live_pps_tick[0]` comes from the dsp\_clk domain.
150	
151	Now the real work begins: now that you know how and where good CDC hygiene
152	has been broken, you should fix it!  For simple command and status bits,
153	it's often enough to just capture them (with the OK1 topology drawn above)
154	into the domain in which they are used.  Other cases may be harder to
155	accomplish without continuing the already-present (in the BAD net)
156	risk of data corruption.  [Gray codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gray_code)
157	are often helpful, as are digital logic design textbooks. Good luck!
158	
159	## Dependencies
160	
161	Besides [cdc\_snitch.py](cdc_snitch.py) and its associated yosys command script
162	[cdc\_snitch\_proc.ys](cdc_snitch_proc.ys), you need:
163	
164	* [yosys](https://yosyshq.net/yosys/) 0.23 or higher
165	* python3
166	* python3 json module
167	
168	## Discussion
169	
170	See [CDC detection with yosys](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/discussions/3956),
171	a discussion started (with prototype code) on Sep 25, 2023.
172	
173	This utility is definitely useful in its current state, and is deployed
174	in a [CI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Continuous_integration) context
175	for production HDL code.
176	We welcome suggestions for or work on improvements.
177	
178	We're still looking for a permanent name for the attribute marking
179	intentional CDC registers, to replace the placeholder `magic_cdc`.
180	Maybe this interacts with industry standards and/or vendor-specific
181	attributes like `DONT_TOUCH` and `ASYNC_REG`.
182	
183	Some registers will have non-clock inputs other than data (D).
184	That includes clock-enable (E) pins and sometimes synchronous reset (R).
185	Each such input is analyzed independently for clock-domain consistency.
186	That only makes sense, especially for R, if yosys's synthesis
187	results match that of the final (normally vendor-specific) synthesis.
188	We use yosys's "techmap" step, that creates gate-level cells for registers
189	with a type name based around "DFF".  We don't currently check for
190	DFF with asynchronous inputs, that are unlikely to be correctly handled.
191	
192	Besides `cdc_snitch`, vendor tools often come with CDC analysis.
193	For example, Vivado `report_cdc`.
194	On the plus side, they will "understand" vendor-specific primitives,
195	and so can cover more of your design.  On the down side, they will
196	push using vendor-supplied IP, that will keep your design from being
197	easily portable to other chip families.
198	Having two CDC analysis tools is better than one!
199

file.content:
# cdc\_snitch Documentation

`cdc_snitch.py` is a python script which, combined with
[yosys](https://yosyshq.net/yosys/) processing of Verilog files,
can detect anti-patterns of Clock-Domain-Crossing (CDC).

We use yosys to synthesize and break down the design to registers,
combinational logic, and memories.  Using json as an intermediate
file format, `cdc_snitch.py` categorizes each register by how its
clock domain does or doesn't match the clock domains of its data sources.

## OK1

![OK1 topology](cdc_OK1.svg)

If all inputs leading to a register are in the same domain as its own
clock, that's called "OK1".  This should be the standard topology for all
real computation and state-machines.

## OKX or CDC

![OKX topology](cdc_OKX.svg)

If each input comes from a single register in another domain, so the
combinational logic is a trivial 'straight wire', that's called "OKX" or "CDC".
All real CDC circuits should be built with this topology at their core.
The default category is "OKX"; if the register has a magic CDC attribute,
then it gets labeled "CDC".  That attribute is intended to mark
_intentional_ CDC crossings.  `cdc_snitch` itself has no way to know
if such intentional CDC crossings are properly designed to avoid
data corruption.

## BAD

![BAD topology](cdc_BAD.svg)

Registers with combinational input, that get at least one input not from
its own domain, are categorized as "BAD".
Maybe this rule could be relaxed some for an ASIC, but in an FPGA
there are no expectations that the output of a LUT will be glitch-free
when inputs change.

Designs with a non-zero number of BAD registers cause `cdc_snitch.py` to
return with an error, as is relevant for a regression-testing makefile.

## Memories

Memories are a special case.  If used to cross clock domains,
the general case would necessarily mark them as "BAD" because
the inputs to the output register include the memory cells
(in the input domain) and address bits (in the output domain).
But when used properly, a data word is not written to and read from
at the same time.
So `cdc_snitch` ignores what goes on inside dual-port memories.

## I/O

Each top-level input port is placed in its own domain,
and output ports are not checked for errors.
This may or may not represent the demands of a specific use case.
We have written shells around the logic-under-test, that add
input and output registers to define the domain of each I/O signal.
If an input _does_ represent a physical pin, the own-domain rule
will give reasonable results.  Such a design will normally capture
the input in a simple register (placed by the synthesizer in the I/O cell),
and that's a valid "OKX" or (with attribute) "CDC".

## Verilog input

Because we call on yosys to process the source Verilog, that Verilog
needs to be portable and synthesizable, and not instantiate (closed-source,
non-synthesizable) vendor primitives.  For many reasons, we try to layer
our designs to put such constructions in an outer chip-specific layer,
with the Real Work instantiated as a portable Verilog module on the inside.
You would then point `cdc_snitch` at that inner layer.

Of course, `cdc_snitch` itself doesn't know or care what language
you feed into yosys.  Verilog and a limited subset of SystemVerilog are OK with yosys-0.23.

## Tool flow

Our makefiles specify rules based on the following
```
foo_yosys.json: foo_shell.v foo.v worker1.v worker2.v $(BUILD_DIR)/cdc_snitch_proc.ys
	$(YOSYS) -p "read_verilog  $(filter %.v, $^); script $(filter %_proc.ys, $^); write_json $@"
foo_cdc.txt: $(BUILD_DIR)/cdc_snitch.py foo_yosys.json
	$(PYTHON) $^ -o $@
```
The chatter from that last command ends with a line like
```
OK1: 81504  CDC: 337  OKX: 1105  BAD: 97
```
counting the number of registers in each category.
The `foo_cdc.txt` file gives details, which ideally can help you find the
design errors that led to the "BAD" registers.
If the design includes any such "BAD" registers, the makefile rule for
`foo_cdc.txt` will fail, as would be used for a regression test.

## Reading the output

It's recommended to save the output of `cdc_snitch.py` in a file by
using the `-o` flag.
Call the result `foo_cdc.txt`, consistent with the make rule above.
That will usually be a big file!  But it is text, and can be understood
as described here.

Ideally, the BAD count is zero, and you don't need to look at `foo_cdc.txt`.
Otherwise, the best way to start is with
```
grep BAD foo_cdc.txt
```
Giving one line per DFF that falls in the BAD category described above.
Each of those lines will look something like
```
BAD  31049 dsp.reg_bank_2[0]:D clk lb_clk inputs ( 8 x lb_clk, 3 x dsp_clk, 1 x dsp.evr_rx_out_clk )
```
Here `dsp.reg_bank_2[0]:D` tells you that the offending path is the D input
to the 0 element of word full of DFF named (by yosys) as `dsp.reg_bank_2`.
Each net often has many different possible names, but you should be able to
find the `reg_bank_2` signal in instance `dsp` in your code.

Then it says `clk lb_clk` identifying the clock to the DFF.
Finally `inputs ( 8 x lb_clk, 3 x dsp_clk, 1 x dsp.evr_rx_out_clk )`
is a count of signals per clock domain feeding the logic.
The goal is to reduce these inputs to a single clock domain,
as described in case OK1 above.

To dive deeper, using that same example, read the whole `foo_cdc.txt`
file with `less` or your favorite text editor.  Now search for the
line discovered above with `grep`.  It is followed by more information
about that BAD DFF input.
```
BAD  31049 dsp.reg_bank_2[0]:D clk lb_clk inputs ( 8 x lb_clk, 3 x dsp_clk, 1 x dsp.evr_rx_out_clk )
  tree 31049 from 397 clk lb_clk name lb_addr_r[0]
  tree 31049 from 398 clk lb_clk name lb_addr_r[1]
  tree 31049 from 399 clk lb_clk name lb_addr_r[2]
  tree 31049 from 400 clk lb_clk name lb_addr_r[3]
  tree 31049 from 18908 clk lb_clk name dsp.fcnt_dsp_clk.work.frequency[0]
  tree 31049 from 28493 clk lb_clk name dsp.timing.evr_timestamp_valid
  tree 31049 from 28547 clk dsp_clk name dsp.evr_live_pps_tick[0]
  tree 31049 from 29201 clk dsp_clk name dsp.timing.i_oc_sync.data_out[0]
  tree 31049 from 29265 clk dsp_clk name dsp.timing.i_oc_sync.data_out[32]
  tree 31049 from 29529 clk dsp.evr_rx_out_clk name dsp.timing.i_evrAROC.evrSROCsynced
  tree 31049 from 29536 clk lb_clk name dsp.timing.i_evcnt_sync.data_out[0]
  tree 31049 from 29724 clk lb_clk name dsp.timing.fcnt_evr_clk.work.frequency[0]
```
Now instead of just a count, you can see each input to the logic cloud
identified by name and clock domain.  So `lb_addr_r[0]` comes from the
lb\_clk domain, and `dsp.evr_live_pps_tick[0]` comes from the dsp\_clk domain.

Now the real work begins: now that you know how and where good CDC hygiene
has been broken, you should fix it!  For simple command and status bits,
it's often enough to just capture them (with the OK1 topology drawn above)
into the domain in which they are used.  Other cases may be harder to
accomplish without continuing the already-present (in the BAD net)
risk of data corruption.  [Gray codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gray_code)
are often helpful, as are digital logic design textbooks. Good luck!

## Dependencies

Besides [cdc\_snitch.py](cdc_snitch.py) and its associated yosys command script
[cdc\_snitch\_proc.ys](cdc_snitch_proc.ys), you need:

* [yosys](https://yosyshq.net/yosys/) 0.23 or higher
* python3
* python3 json module

## Discussion

See [CDC detection with yosys](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/discussions/3956),
a discussion started (with prototype code) on Sep 25, 2023.

This utility is definitely useful in its current state, and is deployed
in a [CI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Continuous_integration) context
for production HDL code.
We welcome suggestions for or work on improvements.

We're still looking for a permanent name for the attribute marking
intentional CDC registers, to replace the placeholder `magic_cdc`.
Maybe this interacts with industry standards and/or vendor-specific
attributes like `DONT_TOUCH` and `ASYNC_REG`.

Some registers will have non-clock inputs other than data (D).
That includes clock-enable (E) pins and sometimes synchronous reset (R).
Each such input is analyzed independently for clock-domain consistency.
That only makes sense, especially for R, if yosys's synthesis
results match that of the final (normally vendor-specific) synthesis.
We use yosys's "techmap" step, that creates gate-level cells for registers
with a type name based around "DFF".  We don't currently check for
DFF with asynchronous inputs, that are unlikely to be correctly handled.

Besides `cdc_snitch`, vendor tools often come with CDC analysis.
For example, Vivado `report_cdc`.
On the plus side, they will "understand" vendor-specific primitives,
and so can cover more of your design.  On the down side, they will
push using vendor-supplied IP, that will keep your design from being
easily portable to other chip families.
Having two CDC analysis tools is better than one!

content.content:
1	"""Analyze Clock-Domain-Crossings (CDC)
2	"""
3	# Pretty crude WIP.  Has some "issues".
4	
5	# Tested Yosys commands to create a compatible json file are
6	# kept nearby in cdc_snitch_proc.ys.
7	
8	# See "CDC detection with yosys" #3956
9	# https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/discussions/3956
10	
11	import json
12	
13	
14	# This is a lot of globals.
15	# Maybe a python expert would like to convert this into a class.
16	xref = {}
17	driver = {}
18	driverless = {}
19	clocks = {}
20	netnames = {}
21	magic_list = {}
22	module_input = {}
23	active_nets = {}
24	ok1_count = 0
25	cdc_count = 0
26	okx_count = 0
27	bad_count = 0
28	MAGIC_CDC = "magic_cdc"  # placeholder for attribute name
29	
30	
31	def summarize(dom_list):
32	    aa = {}
33	    for b in dom_list:
34	        if b in aa:
35	            aa[b] += 1
36	        else:
37	            aa[b] = 1
38	    summary = ["%d x %s" % (aa[b], get_clk_name(b)) for b in sorted(aa.keys())]
39	    return ", ".join(summary)
40	
41	
42	def check_bit(dout_num, dout_name, clk, magic, inputs, ofile, verbose=True):
43	    global ok1_count, cdc_count, okx_count, bad_count, active_nets
44	    dom_in = []
45	    active_nets = {}
46	    dout_num = str(dout_num)
47	    for p in inputs:
48	        dom_in += list_domains(p)
49	    for d in dom_in:
50	        clocks[d] = True
51	    if all([d == clk for d in dom_in]):
52	        stat = "OK1"  # all inputs in same domain as the DFF
53	        ok1_count += 1
54	        if magic and len(dom_in) > 1:
55	            print('warning: misplaced CDC attribute on', dout_name)
56	    elif len(dom_in) == 1:
57	        if magic:
58	            # planned, single input, no combinational logic
59	            stat = "CDC"
60	            cdc_count += 1
61	        else:
62	            # unplanned, single input, no combinational logic, maybe OK
63	            stat = "OKX"
64	            okx_count += 1
65	    else:
66	        # doesn't matter if they claim CDC or not, it's still bad
67	        stat = "BAD"  # no way
68	        bad_count += 1
69	    mm = "magic" if magic else ""
70	    clk_name = get_clk_name(clk)
71	    pl = [stat, mm, dout_num, dout_name, 'clk', clk_name, 'inputs (', summarize(dom_in), ')']
72	    ofile.write(" ".join(pl) + "\n")
73	    if verbose and stat == "BAD":
74	        for ix in sorted(active_nets.keys()):
75	            px = []
76	            if ix in driver:
77	                direct = driver[ix]
78	                conns = direct['connections']
79	                type_name = direct['type']
80	                if "DFF" in type_name:
81	                    dom = conns['C'][0]
82	                    dom_name = get_clk_name(dom)
83	                    px = ["clk", dom_name, "name", netnames[ix]]
84	            elif ix in module_input:
85	                # second priority to avoid getting confused by inout
86	                px = ["modinput", netnames[ix]]
87	            else:
88	                px = ["unknown"]
89	            if px:
90	                pl = ["  tree", dout_num, "from", str(ix)] + px
91	                ofile.write(" ".join(pl) + "\n")
92	
93	
94	def find_dff(mod, ofile):
95	    for cell in mod['cells'].keys():
96	        # print(cell)
97	        # print(mod['cells'][cell])
98	        c = mod['cells'][cell]
99	        type_name = c['type']
100	        # print(cell, type_name)
101	        if "DFF" in type_name:
102	            conns = c['connections']
103	            #
104	            # C = clock defining output domain
105	            # Should really check that the port_direction for C is "input",
106	            # and its list of connections is exactly 1 long.
107	            if len(conns["C"]) != 1:
108	                print("warning: multiple clocks", cell)
109	            clk = conns["C"][0]
110	            clocks[clk] = True
111	            #
112	            # Q = dout used for naming
113	            # Should really check that the port_direction for Q is "output",
114	            if len(conns["Q"]) != 1:
115	                print("warning: multiple clocks", cell)
116	            dout = conns["Q"][0]  # ditto about multiple Q
117	            magic = dout in magic_list
118	            #
119	            # Treat D, CE, and CLR pins independently.
120	            # Tacit assumption that the underlying FPGA cells have these features
121	            # built-in, in a way that can't/won't be messed up by the place&route.
122	            for conn in conns:
123	                if c['port_directions'][conn] == 'input' and conn != "C":
124	                    # print('tracking port', conn, conns[conn])
125	                    inputs = conns[conn]
126	                    use_magic = magic and conn == "D"
127	                    check_bit(dout, netnames[dout]+":"+conn, clk, use_magic, inputs, ofile)
128	        elif "memwr_v2" in type_name:
129	            memid = "unknown"
130	            if "MEMID" in c["parameters"]:
131	                memid = c["parameters"]["MEMID"][1:]
132	            # print("debug1", type_name, cell, memid)
133	            conns = c['connections']
134	            # for pp in conns.keys():
135	            #     print("debug2", pp, c["port_directions"][pp], len(conns[pp]))
136	            clk = conns["CLK"][0]
137	            # treat each data slice independently
138	            addrs = conns["ADDR"]
139	            for ix, din in enumerate(conns["DATA"]):
140	                inputs = [din] + addrs + [conns["EN"][ix]]
141	                dout_name = memid + "[%d]" % ix
142	                # print("debug3", dout_name, inputs)
143	                # XXX on dout
144	                check_bit(0, dout_name, clk, False, inputs, ofile)
145	
146	
147	# recursively calls itself
148	def list_domains(ix):
149	    if ix == "1" or ix == "0" or ix == "x" or ix == "z":
150	        return []  # without complaint, since this is a known case
151	    if ix in active_nets:  # already got here
152	        return []
153	    active_nets[ix] = True
154	    if ix in module_input:
155	        # print("module_input", ix)
156	        # treat each module input as if it were its own clock domain
157	        return [ix]
158	    if ix not in driver:
159	        # print("driverless", ix, netnames[ix])
160	        driverless[ix] = True
161	        return []
162	    direct = driver[ix]
163	    conns = direct['connections']
164	    type_name = direct['type']
165	    # print('list_domains start', ix, type_name)
166	    if "DFF" in type_name:
167	        dom = [conns['C'][0]]
168	    else:
169	        # couple of list comprehensions
170	        pd = direct['port_directions']
171	        l1 = [conn for conn in conns if (pd[conn] == 'input')]
172	        # print(l1)
173	        l2 = [conns[conn] for conn in l1]
174	        c1 = sum(l2, [])
175	        # print(c1)
176	        dom = sum([list_domains(cc) for cc in c1], [])
177	    # print('list_domains done', ix, type_name, dom)
178	    return dom
179	
180	
181	def get_clk_name(nbit):
182	    return netnames.get(nbit, str(nbit))
183	
184	
185	def index_netnames(mod):
186	    for nn in mod['netnames'].keys():
187	        attr = mod['netnames'][nn]['attributes']
188	        bits = mod['netnames'][nn]['bits']
189	        if len(bits) == 1:
190	            netnames[bits[0]] = nn
191	        else:
192	            for ix, b in enumerate(bits):
193	                netnames[b] = nn + ("[%d]" % ix)
194	        if MAGIC_CDC in attr:
195	            for b in bits:
196	                magic_list[b] = True
197	
198	
199	def index_drivers(mod):
200	    for cell in mod['cells'].keys():
201	        c = mod['cells'][cell]
202	        # type_name = c['type']
203	        for conn in c['connections'].keys():
204	            if c['port_directions'][conn] == "output":
205	                for ix in c['connections'][conn]:
206	                    # print('connection found', ix, c['type'])
207	                    if ix in driver:
208	                        print("colliding drivers for", ix,
209	                              c['type'], c['attributes'], 'and',
210	                              driver[ix]['type'], driver[ix]['attributes'])
211	                    else:
212	                        driver[ix] = c
213	    for port in mod['ports'].keys():
214	        p = mod['ports'][port]
215	        # print("debug", port, p['direction'])
216	        if p['direction'] == 'input' or p['direction'] == 'inout':
217	            for ix in p['bits']:
218	                module_input[ix] = True
219	
220	
221	def sift_design(yosys_json, ofile):
222	    creator = yosys_json['creator']
223	    print("json from", creator)
224	    # maybe we should bark if yosys version is less than 0.23
225	    modules = yosys_json['modules']
226	    module_set = sorted(list(modules.keys()))
227	    print("modules:", " ".join(module_set))
228	    if len(module_set) != 1:
229	        print('too many modules; try yosys command "flatten -wb <top_module>"')
230	        return 1
231	    module1 = modules[module_set[0]]
232	    index_netnames(module1)
233	    index_drivers(module1)
234	    find_dff(module1, ofile)
235	    return 0
236	
237	
238	def sift_file(f, ofile, strict=False):
239	    rc = sift_design(json.load(f), ofile)
240	    if rc != 0:
241	        return rc
242	    if False:
243	        for b in sorted(driver.keys()):
244	            print(b, driver[b]['type'])
245	    if True:
246	        for b in sorted(driverless.keys()):
247	            print('driverless', b, netnames[b])
248	    if False:
249	        domain_list = sorted(clocks.keys())
250	        print('clock domains', domain_list)
251	        for d in domain_list:
252	            print('net', d, 'name', netnames[d])
253	    pp = (ok1_count, cdc_count, okx_count, bad_count)
254	    print("OK1: %d  CDC: %d  OKX: %d  BAD: %d" % pp)
255	    if bad_count != 0:
256	        rc = 1
257	    if strict and okx_count != 0:
258	        rc = 1
259	    return rc
260	
261	
262	if __name__ == "__main__":
263	    import argparse
264	    from sys import stdout
265	    hint = "Looks through a yosys-output json file and categorizes CDCs.\n"
266	    hint += "Longer documentation in cdc_snitch.md."
267	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__, epilog=hint,
268	                                     formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter)
269	    parser.add_argument("-o", "--output", dest='ofile', default=None,
270	                        help="Output file")
271	    parser.add_argument("--strict", default=False, action="store_true",
272	                        help="Strict checking: all CDC must be marked")
273	    parser.add_argument("file", help="json input file, as generated by yosys")
274	    args = parser.parse_args()
275	    rc = 1
276	    if args.ofile is not None:
277	        ofile = open(args.ofile, "w")
278	    else:
279	        ofile = stdout
280	    with open(args.file, "r") as f:
281	        rc = sift_file(f, ofile, strict=args.strict)
282	    exit(rc)
283

1	# Yosys script to convert a design to flat gates and memories,
2	# in support of the clock-domain-crossing analysis performed by cdc_snitch.py.
3	
4	# See "CDC detection with yosys" #3956
5	# https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/discussions/3956
6	
7	# You need a mechanism outside this script to do the read_verilog
8	# and write_json steps.  Unix/make users should be able to accomplish that
9	# with a makefile action like
10	#    $(YOSYS) -p "read_verilog $(filter %.v, $^); script cdc_snitch_proc.ys; write_json $@"
11	
12	# only checked with yosys-0.23
13	
14	hierarchy -simcheck -auto-top
15	
16	select t:$meminit_v2
17	delete
18	select -clear
19	
20	proc
21	opt -purge
22	stat
23	
24	select t:$memrd t:$memrd_v2 t:$memwr_v2
25	setattr -set keep_hierarchy 1
26	select -clear
27	
28	flatten -wb
29	opt -fast -purge
30	synth -run :coarse
31	techmap
32	# the following sub-steps are a good-enough (for this purpose) equivalent to
33	#   opt -fast -purge
34	# but run much faster
35	opt_expr
36	opt_merge
37	opt_reduce
38	opt_dff
39	opt_clean -purge
40	opt_expr
41	opt_clean -purge
42

44	stat

file.content:
# Yosys script to convert a design to flat gates and memories,
# in support of the clock-domain-crossing analysis performed by cdc_snitch.py.

# See "CDC detection with yosys" #3956
# https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/discussions/3956

# You need a mechanism outside this script to do the read_verilog
# and write_json steps.  Unix/make users should be able to accomplish that
# with a makefile action like
#    $(YOSYS) -p "read_verilog $(filter %.v, $^); script cdc_snitch_proc.ys; write_json $@"

# only checked with yosys-0.23

hierarchy -simcheck -auto-top

select t:$meminit_v2
delete
select -clear

proc
opt -purge
stat

select t:$memrd t:$memrd_v2 t:$memwr_v2
setattr -set keep_hierarchy 1
select -clear

flatten -wb
opt -fast -purge
synth -run :coarse
techmap
# the following sub-steps are a good-enough (for this purpose) equivalent to
#   opt -fast -purge
# but run much faster
opt_expr
opt_merge
opt_reduce
opt_dff
opt_clean -purge
opt_expr
opt_clean -purge

hierarchy -check
stat
check

content.content:
1	# Build flags for all targets
2	GCC_FLAGS = -Wstrict-prototypes -Wpointer-arith -Wcast-align -Wcast-qual \
3		-Wshadow -Waggregate-return -Wmissing-prototypes -Wnested-externs \
4		-Wall -W -Wno-unused -Winline -Wwrite-strings -Wundef -pedantic
5	GCC_FLAGS += -Wformat -Wformat-signedness
6	CF_ALL = -Wall -O2 -fPIC -g -std=c99 -D_DEFAULT_SOURCE $(GCC_FLAGS) ${CFLAGS_$@}
7	LF_ALL = ${LDFLAGS_$@}
8	
9	ICARUS_SUFFIX =
10	VERILOG_VPI = iverilog-vpi$(ICARUS_SUFFIX)
11	VERILOG = iverilog$(ICARUS_SUFFIX) -Wall -Wno-macro-redefinition
12	VG_ALL = -DSIMULATE
13	V_TB = -Wno-timescale
14	VFLAGS = ${VFLAGS_$@} -I$(AUTOGEN_DIR)
15	VVP_FLAGS = ${VVP_FLAGS_$@}
16	VVP = vvp$(ICARUS_SUFFIX) -N
17	VERILATOR = verilator -Wall -Wno-fatal
18	GTKWAVE = gtkwave
19	VPIEXT = vpi
20	PYTHON = python3
21	PERL = perl
22	AWK = awk
23	XCIRCUIT = xcircuit
24	SV2V = sv2v
25	YOSYS = yosys
26	YOSYS_QUIET = -q
27	YOSYS_JSON_OPTION = -DBUGGY_FORLOOP
28	# (that flag adjusts the behavior of dpram.v; I don't think yosys for-loop
29	# is actually buggy, just tediously slow, and in this case unnecessary)
30	YOSYS_JSON_PRECHECK = true
31	YOSYS_READ_VERILOG = read_verilog
32	
33	VPI_CFLAGS := $(shell $(VERILOG_VPI) --cflags)
34	VPI_LDFLAGS := $(shell $(VERILOG_VPI) --ldflags)
35	DEPDIR = _dep
36	IPX_DIR = _ipx
37	AUTOGEN_DIR = _autogen
38	CHECK_CLEAN = sh $(BUILD_DIR)/check_clean
39	
40	# Build tools
41	#CC = $(BUILD_DIR)/ccd-gcc
42	CC = gcc
43	INST = $(BUILD_DIR)/install
44	COMP = $(CC) $(CF_ALL) $(CF_TGT) -o $@ -c $<
45	LINK = $(CC) $(LF_ALL) $(LF_TGT) -o $@ $^ $(LL_TGT)
46	COMPLINK = $(CC) $(CF_ALL) $(CF_TGT) $(LF_ALL) $(LF_TGT) -o $@ $< $(LL_TGT)
47	ARCH = ar rcs $@ $^
48	VERILOG_COMP = $(VERILOG) $(VG_ALL) $(VPI_TGT) ${VFLAGS} -o $@ $^
49	VERILOG_TB = $(VERILOG) $(VG_ALL) $(V_TB) ${VFLAGS} -o $@ $(filter %v, $^)
50	VERILOG_TB_VPI = $(VERILOG) $(VG_ALL) $(VPI_TGT) ${VFLAGS} -o $@ $(filter %.v, $^)
51	VERILOG_SIM = cd `dirname $@` && $(VVP) `basename $<` $(VVP_FLAGS)
52	VERILOG_VIEW = $(GTKWAVE) $(GTKW_OPT) $^
53	VERILOG_CHECK = $(VVP) $< $(VVP_FLAGS)
54	# FIXME This hack does not work with vpath
55	VERILOG_TBLINT = $(PYTHON) $(BUILD_DIR)/tblint.py $(if $(realpath $<.v),$<.v,$<.sv)
56	VERILOG_RUN = $(VVP) $@
57	#VPI_LINK = $(VERILOG_VPI) --name=$(basename $@) $^ $(LL_TGT) $(LF_ALL) $(VPI_LDFLAGS)
58	VPI_LINK = $(CXX) -std=gnu99 -o $@ $^ $(LL_TGT) $(LF_ALL) $(VPI_LDFLAGS)
59	MAKEDEP = $(VERILOG) $(V_TB) $(VG_ALL) ${VFLAGS} $(VFLAGS_DEP) -o /dev/null -M$@.$$$$ $<
60	
61	VLATORFLAGS = $(subst -y,-y ,${VFLAGS}) $(subst -y,-y ,${VFLAGS_DEP}) -y . -I.
62	# keep -Wno-TIMESCALEMOD separate, since it's a new flag not supported by Verilator 4.010 in Debian Buster
63	VLATOR_TIMESCALEMOD = -Wno-TIMESCALEMOD
64	# new flag for Verilator v4.226 and beyond
65	VLATOR_TIMING = --timing
66	VLATOR_LINT_IGNORE = -Wno-PINMISSING -Wno-WIDTH -Wno-REDEFMACRO -Wno-PINCONNECTEMPTY $(VLATOR_TIMESCALEMOD)
67	VERILATOR_LINT = $(VERILATOR) $(VG_ALL) ${VLATORFLAGS} ${VLATOR_LINT_IGNORE} --lint-only $(filter %.v %.sv, $^)
68	VERILATOR_MAKEDEP = $(VERILATOR_LINT) -Wno-DECLFILENAME -Wno-UNUSED -Wno-CASEINCOMPLETE -Wno-UNDRIVEN $(VLATOR_TIMING) --MMD --Mdir $(DEPDIR)
69	VERILATOR_SIM = $(VERILATOR) --trace-fst -O2 $(VLATOR_LINT_IGNORE) $(VG_ALL) +define+VERILATOR_SIM
70	
71	FMC_MAP = awk -F\" 'NR==FNR{a[$$2]=$$4;next}$$4 in a{printf "NET %-15s LOC = %-4s | IOSTANDARD = %10s; \# %s\n",$$2,a[$$4],$$6,$$4}'
72	XDC_MAP = awk -F"[ \"\t]+" 'NR==FNR{gsub(/]/,"",$$8);a[$$8]=$$4;next}($$3 in a){printf "set_property -dict \"PACKAGE_PIN %-4s IOSTANDARD %s\" [get_ports %s]\n",a[$$3], $$4, $$2}'
73	ISE_SYNTH = bash $(BUILD_DIR)/xil_syn
74	VIVADO_CMD = vivado -mode batch -nojou -nolog
75	VIVADO_SYNTH = $(VIVADO_CMD) -source $(BUILD_DIR)/vivado_tcl/project_proc.tcl $(BUILD_DIR)/vivado_tcl/vivado_project.tcl -tclargs
76	VIVADO_REMOTE_SYNTH = $(VIVADO_SYNTH)
77	# SYNTH_OPT = -DMEM_SIZE=16384
78	PLANAHEAD_SYNTH = planAhead -mode batch -nojou -nolog -source $(BUILD_DIR)/vivado_tcl/project_proc.tcl $(BUILD_DIR)/vivado_tcl/planahead_project.tcl -tclargs
79	VIVADO_FLASH = $(VIVADO_CMD) -source $(BUILD_DIR)/vivado_tcl/vivado_flash.tcl -tclargs
80	VIVADO_CREATE_IP = $(VIVADO_CMD) -source $(BUILD_DIR)/vivado_tcl/lbl_ip.tcl $(BUILD_DIR)/vivado_tcl/create_ip.tcl -tclargs
81	OCTAVE_SILENT = $(OCTAVE) -q $<
82	PS2PDF = ps2pdf -dEPSCrop $< $@
83	CHECK = $(VVP) $<
84	BIT2RBF = bit2rbf $@ < $<
85	GIT_VERSION = $(shell git describe --abbrev=4 --dirty --always --tags)
86	
87	# General directory-independent implicit rules
88	
89	%.o: %.c
90		$(COMP)
91	
92	%: %.o
93		$(LINK)
94	
95	%: %.c
96		$(COMPLINK)
97	
98	%.a: %.o
99		$(ARCH)
100	
101	# Sorry about the conditional; I couldn't find any other way to make newad work.
102	ifndef NO_DEFAULT_TB_RULE
103	%_tb: %_tb.v
104		$(VERILOG_TB)
105	endif
106	
107	%_live: %_tb.v
108		$(VERILOG_TB)
109	
110	%_tb_vpi: %_tb.v
111		$(VERILOG_TB_VPI)
112	
113	%: %.v
114		$(VERILOG_COMP)
115	
116	%.vpi: %.o
117		$(VPI_LINK)
118	
119	%.vcd: %_tb
120		$(VERILOG_SIM) +vcd $(VCD_ARGS)
121	
122	%_view: %.vcd %.gtkw
123		$(VERILOG_VIEW)
124	
125	%_check: %_tb
126		$(VERILOG_CHECK)
127	
128	%_lint: %.v %_auto
129		$(VERILATOR_LINT)
130	
131	V%_tb: $(wildcard *.sv) $(wildcard *.v)
132		$(VERILATOR_SIM) -I$(V$*_CLIB) -cc --exe $(filter %.v %.sv %.cpp %.c, $^) --top-module $* -y $(AUTOGEN_DIR)
133		MAKEFLAGS="" make -C obj_dir -f V$*.mk USER_CPPFLAGS="-I$(V$*_CLIB)"
134		mv obj_dir/V$* $@
135	
136	%: %.m
137		$(OCTAVE_SILENT)
138	
139	%.pdf: %.eps
140		$(PS2PDF)
141	
142	# Kind of weird to use xcircuit's rc file for this purpose, but it does work.
143	%.svg: %.eps
144		cd $(dir $@) && echo "page load $<; svg; exit" > .xcircuitrc; $(XVFB) $(XCIRCUIT); rm .xcircuitrc
145	
146	%.rbf: %.bit
147		$(BIT2RBF)
148	
149	%.v: %.vpx
150		$(PYTHON) $(BUILD_DIR)/third.py $< > $@
151	
152	%.dot: %.vpx
153		$(PYTHON) $(BUILD_DIR)/third.py -dot $< > $@
154	
155	%.dat: %_tb
156		$(VVP) $< $(VVP_FLAGS) > $@
157	
158	# cdc_snitch
159	%_yosys.json: %.v $(BUILD_DIR)/cdc_snitch_proc.ys
160		$(YOSYS_JSON_PRECHECK)
161		$(YOSYS) --version
162		$(YOSYS) $(YOSYS_QUIET) -p "$(YOSYS_READ_VERILOG) $(YOSYS_JSON_OPTION) $(filter %.v, $^); script $(filter %_proc.ys, $^); write_json $@"
163	
164	%_cdc.txt: $(BUILD_DIR)/cdc_snitch.py %_yosys.json
165		$(PYTHON) $^ -o $@
166	
167	ifeq ($(XILINX_TOOL), VIVADO)
168	%_$(DAUGHTER).xdc: $(BOARD_SUPPORT_DIR)/$(HARDWARE)/%.xdc  $(BOARD_SUPPORT_DIR)/$(DAUGHTER)/fmc.map
